BDRs consistently make a tangible impact in the Sales Development Organization by generating millions of dollars of new business pipeline each year. Additionally, BDRs are enrolled in an intensive sales training program with the purpose of providing new professionals with the fundamental skills necessary to achieve a successful sales career at Salesforce. **Day to Day** * Generate new business pipeline primarily through prospecting outbound opportunities * Manage inbound leads that are driven by outbound effort * Nurture early\-stage opportunities * Gain interest through outbound cold calling across a set of existing Salesforce customers and breaking into net new logos in assigned territory * Partner with core seller to identify and source net new pipeline and assist by researching lines of business and personas * Align with internal account team * Identify key decision makers within new accounts **Preferred Qualifications** * Fluency in Arabic and English is required for this role. * Experience in business development, sales or related field experience * Ability to work in a fast paced environment and across many different parts of the organisation to get things done * Experience researching, account planning, prospecting, and cold calling into a large list of new and existing accounts * Proven track record of achieving sales metrics and consistent achievement of year\-over\-year quota attainment * Degree or equivalent relevant experience required. Experience will be evaluated based on the core competencies for the role (e.g. extracurricular leadership roles, military experience, volunteer roles, work experience, etc.) **Working at Salesforce** Working at Salesforce isn’t all about selling. It’s also about learning, and we heavily invest in you with a month\-long immersion and onboarding, including: a week\-long product bootcamp, mentorship program, weekly coaching and development programs. **Benefits** We are pioneers of the Pledge 1% model, providing product, grants and community service to those in need. We are proud to be\#1 in PEOPLE's **Top 50 Companies that Care** , and are on Fortune’s **Change the World** list. We provide every employee with 7 paid volunteer days off a year, and donation matching for all approved charitable donations. We provide other world\-leading benefits to all our employees, including; * Health, life insurance, retirement saving plan * Monthly wellness allowance * Flexible time off \& leave policies * Parental benefits * Perks and discounts * For more information, check out visit https://www.getsalesforcebenefits.com/en for the full breakdown! We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. Please contact us to request accommodation. **Posting Statement** At Salesforce we believe that the business of business is to improve the state of our world. Each of us has a responsibility to drive Equality in our communities and workplaces. We are committed to creating a workforce that reflects society through inclusive programs and initiatives such as equal pay, employee resource groups, inclusive benefits, and more. Learn more about Equality at Salesforce and explore our benefits. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org are Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employers. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org do not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org will not pay any third\-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Salesforce.com or Salesforce.org. Salesforce welcomes all. More details about our company benefits can be found at the following link: https://www.getsalesforcebenefits.com/ Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and the Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring, Salesforce will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Butcher Assistant
description: **Butcher Assistant** Are you passionate about food and enjoy working with people? We’re looking for a motivated and customer\-focused **Butcher Assistant** to join our team. This is a fantastic opportunity to work in a fast\-paced, hands\-on environment where you’ll play a key role in delivering high\-quality products and excellent service to our customers. No previous experience? No problem — full training will be provided to help you succeed. **About the Role** You’ll help ensure the Meat Department runs smoothly and efficiently while maintaining the highest standards of quality, freshness, and presentation. You’ll also create a welcoming and helpful experience for every customer. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Deliver friendly, knowledgeable, and attentive customer service * Prepare and serve meat products to customer specifications * Maintain high standards of merchandising and product presentation * Ensure stock is rotated correctly to guarantee freshness and quality * Follow all SOPs and store standards on a daily basis * Keep the department clean, organised, and fully compliant * Stay up to date with new products and initiatives * Share ideas and contribute to improving the department * Embrace new ways of working and support team success **What We’re Looking For** * Strong communication skills and a positive attitude * A customer\-first mindset * A genuine passion for food and fresh products * Ability to work as part of a team in a busy environment * Comfortable multitasking and working under pressure * No experience required — just a willingness to learn **Job Type**: Part Time **Work authorisation**: Ireland (required) **Work Location**: In person **Benefits** * Bike to Work Scheme * Company Pension * Employee Assistance Programme * Employee Discount * Store Discount Pay: From €14\.15 per hour Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * Store discount Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Chemistry Expert (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Chemistry Expert (AI Training)** **About The Role** What if your years of chemistry expertise could directly shape how AI reasons through the most complex scientific problems in the world? We're looking for Chemistry Experts with advanced degrees to design, solve, and evaluate graduate\-level chemistry problems that help train cutting\-edge AI models to think like scientists. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role built for working researchers, postdocs, faculty, and industry scientists who want meaningful work on their own schedule. No prior AI experience needed — just deep chemistry knowledge and a commitment to scientific rigor. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Design and solve advanced chemistry problems spanning organic, inorganic, physical, and computational chemistry * Review and evaluate AI\-generated scientific content for accuracy, depth, and reasoning quality * Develop complex, real\-world problem statements that challenge and improve AI model reasoning * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and fellow domain experts * Ensure scientific rigor, clarity, and precision across all deliverables * Apply computational tools or coding skills where relevant to problem design and validation **Who You Are** * Hold a Master's or PhD in Chemistry or a closely related field * Deep expertise in at least one area: organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Comfortable working with Python or computational chemistry software (e.g., Gaussian, VASP) * Exceptionally detail\-oriented with strong scientific communication skills * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently and asynchronously * Fluent in English **Nice to Have** * Experience with data annotation, scientific evaluation, or quality assurance workflows * Background in AI, machine learning, or cheminformatics * Familiarity with molecular modeling, reaction mechanisms, or materials science * Experience writing for scientific publication or peer review **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside leading research labs and AI teams * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Freelance autonomy with the structure of meaningful, task\-based scientific work * Apply your expertise to problems that directly influence how AI understands science * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Chief Financial Officer
description: ***Fire. Chief Financial Officer (CFO) \- Dublin*** **What we do?** Fire is an embedded payments services provider. We empower businesses to get paid, make payments, and automate their payment processes by offering integrated access to a range of payment services. Regulated in both Ireland and the UK, Fire is a member of multiple payment schemes and networks across the UK and Europe. As an established payment services provider, we work with thousands of businesses and some of the region’s largest financial institutions to co\-create and deliver innovative payment solutions that drive efficiency, scalability, and customer value. **What is the role?** We are seeking a Chief Financial Officer (“CFO”) for the Group. As a member of the senior management team and reporting to the CEO, the successful candidate will play a key role in the overall management and strategic direction of the business. This role will hold responsibility for PCF 11 (Head of Finance) and PCF 56 (Head of Safeguarding), subject to regulatory approval, and is suited to an individual looking to step into a PCF position for the first time. The role requires a forward\-thinking individual who can lead our financial strategy while managing risk in a high\-growth environment. The CFO will be responsible for overseeing the company’s financial activities and ensuring timely and accurate reporting to the CEO and the Board of Directors, while building capability in line with regulatory expectations. The role entails: * **Financial Control –** maintain and optimise the financial operations (provided via an outsourced accounting function) ensuring timely and accurate reporting of the management accounts and other key metrics of the business. * **Business Planning** \- responsible for the creation, ongoing management and reporting of a robust business planning framework including forecasting and stress testing. * **MIS and Insights –** produce quality reporting on both a regular and ad\-hoc basis providing commercial insights supporting business decisions. * **Treasury –** oversee the company’s cash flow ensuring there are strong controls around banking, payables, receivables and interest income. * **Tax** – maintain appropriate structures and ensure constant compliance. * **Audits** – lead the annual financial audit and provide support in the completion of other audits and external assessments. * **Legal** – final sign off on all legal documentation, new and renewal contracts, commercial and corporate. * **HR** – manage the HR function ensuring there are a robust set of policies for recruiting, development and creating a framework that optimises performance in line with targets consistent with the strategy. * **Facilities \& Office Management** – management of the office locations (Dublin and London) and all supporting services. * **Governance –** maintain robust financial controls and governance frameworks. * **Regulatory –** ultimate ownership of all regulatory reports. * **Engagement (internal)** – provide support to other functions and lead in the production of quality reports to the management team and Board of Directors. * **Engagement (external)** – develop and maintain relationships with external parties including banking partners, outsourced accounting, tax \& legal advisors, customers and suppliers. **What should you have?** * Qualified accountant with c. 5\+ years’ experience, ideally with exposure to senior finance responsibilities. * Demonstrated capability and readiness to step into a Pre\-Approval Control Function (PCF) role, meeting regulator Fitness \& Probity requirements. * Strong financial and analytical skills, with good commercial awareness and an ability to support business decision\-making. * Ability to produce high\-quality work with strong attention to detail. * Excellent interpersonal, communication and organisational skills. * An understanding of our product, or a strong interest in learning, and enthusiasm to be part of a scaling team. **What else?** You will be joining a fast\-growing established fintech business with offices in Dublin and London. You will have the opportunity to learn, develop and make an impact. In addition to offering a competitive salary and share reward scheme we will provide you with a great place to work, engage and take responsibility for delivering compelling financial services products. This role is based in Dublin and operates three days a week in the office and two days working from home. Fire Financial Services Limited (Fire\-EU) – trading as **fire.com** – is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland as a Payment Institution (No: C58301\) and Fire Financial Services Limited (Fire\-UK) is authorised as an Electronic Money Institution by the Financial Conduct Authority (No: 900983\) For more information on our privacy statement, please refer to **https://www.fire.com/privacy\-statement\-candidates**

title: Chief Operating Officer
description: **Chief Operating Officer (COO)** Location: Dublin, Ireland (Global remit – supporting Ireland, UK, US, Chennai, Grenada, Australia, New Zealand, and additional markets) Reporting to: Chief Executive Officer (CEO) **About Foodhub** Foodhub is a fast\-growing global technology company entering an exciting new phase of expansion. With a newly established Dublin headquarters and operations across the UK, USA, ANZ and India, we are building a high\-calibre senior leadership team to help shape the future of the business. This is a rare opportunity to join at a pivotal stage — the energy and ambition of a scale\-up, backed by the stability and success of an established international business. Foodhub is profitable, debt\-free, and has delivered sustained year\-on\-year growth across multiple international markets. As we accelerate our global expansion strategy through both organic growth and international acquisitions, we are looking for ambitious leaders who want to play a defining role in building something significant. This is more than a leadership position — it’s an opportunity to influence the direction of a dynamic global business at the start of its next major growth chapter. **Overview** Foodhub is seeking a highly experienced and commercially driven Chief Operating Officer (COO) to lead and scale our global operations. This is a critical executive role responsible for ensuring operational excellence, driving efficiency, and aligning business functions to support Foodhub’s continued growth and international expansion. Working closely with the CEO and executive leadership team, the COO will oversee day\-to\-day business operations across all regions, ensuring consistent execution of company strategy. The role requires a strong operator with a track record of managing complex, multi\-market organisations, optimising performance, and building high\-performing teams. This role will involve **regular international travel** across Foodhub’s global markets to ensure alignment, performance, and strong local leadership engagement. **Key Responsibilities** **Operational Leadership \& Strategy** • Lead global operations, ensuring alignment with Foodhub’s strategic objectives and growth plans. • Translate company vision into actionable operational plans with clear KPIs and accountability. • Drive operational efficiency, scalability, and continuous improvement across all markets. • Partner with the CEO and executive team to shape and execute company\-wide strategy. **Global Business Operations** • Oversee day\-to\-day operations across all regions, ensuring consistency, performance, and compliance. • Optimise processes, systems, and workflows to support rapid growth and operational resilience. • Ensure seamless coordination between departments including technology, customer support, finance, and commercial teams. • Monitor operational performance and implement improvements to enhance productivity and service delivery. **Performance \& Delivery** • Establish and track key operational metrics, ensuring strong performance management across the business. • Drive accountability across regional and functional leaders to deliver on targets. • Identify risks and opportunities, taking proactive steps to mitigate issues and improve outcomes. • Lead regular business reviews with senior stakeholders. **People \& Leadership** • Build, lead, and develop a high\-performing global operations team. • Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, and operational excellence. • Support leadership development and succession planning across key operational roles. • Act as a key advisor to the CEO and leadership team on organisational design and operational structure. **Process, Systems \& Infrastructure** • Oversee the development and implementation of scalable systems and operational frameworks. • Ensure robust infrastructure to support global growth and new market entry. • Champion the use of data, automation, and technology to improve efficiency and decision\-making. **Financial \& Commercial Oversight** • Work closely with the finance team to manage budgets, cost control, and operational profitability. • Identify opportunities to improve margins and optimise resource allocation. • Support commercial decision\-making with operational insights and data. **Governance \& Compliance** • Ensure operational compliance across all jurisdictions, working with legal and regional teams. • Establish strong governance frameworks and risk management processes. • Maintain high standards of operational integrity and accountability. **Qualifications \& Experience** • Proven experience as a COO or in a senior operational leadership role within a large\-scale, global organisation (preferably 1,000\+ employees). • **Direct experience within the food ordering, hospitality technology, or POS/EPOS industry is essential.** • Strong background in scaling operations across multiple countries and regions. • Experience within technology, platform businesses, or high\-growth environments is highly desirable. • Demonstrated success in driving operational efficiency, transformation, and business growth. • Strong financial acumen with experience managing budgets and performance metrics. • Track record of leading large, diverse, and geographically distributed teams. • Exceptional stakeholder management and ability to influence at board and executive level. • Experience building and implementing scalable systems, processes, and infrastructure. • Degree in business, operations, or a related field (MBA or equivalent preferred). **Key Skills \& Attributes** • Strategic and commercially minded operator with strong execution capability. • Results\-driven with a focus on performance, efficiency, and accountability. • Excellent leadership and team\-building skills. • Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities. • Highly organised with the ability to manage complexity and competing priorities. • Resilient, adaptable, and comfortable in a fast\-paced, high\-growth environment. • Strong communication and interpersonal skills across all levels of the organisation. • Culturally aware with experience working across global markets. • Willingness and ability to travel internationally on a regular basis. **Benefits** • Competitive compensation and executive benefits package. • Opportunity to play a key leadership role in a fast\-growing global technology company. • Direct partnership with CEO and executive leadership team. • Dynamic and collaborative work environment with significant impact and ownership. • 33 days annual leave per year (including public holidays). • Option to buy up to 5 additional annual leave days per year. • Guaranteed day off on your birthday (when requested). • Option to purchase Bupa Select Complete Health Insurance (Medical History Disregarded) for self and family at a significantly discounted rate. • €35 monthly gym contribution.

customer\-centric solutions. **Engineering \& Team Leadership** • Build, lead, and scale a high\-performing global engineering and technology organisation. • Foster a culture of innovation, markets. • Implement best\-in\-class DevOps, cloud, and system monitoring practices. • Manage risk and ensure business continuity through strong technical governance. **Data \& Technology Enablement** • Champion data\-driven decision\-making across the organisation. • Oversee data architecture, analytics capabilities, and platform integrations. • Drive the use of automation, AI, and emerging technologies to improve efficiency and customer experience. **Process, Systems \& Transformation** • Establish scalable development processes, tools, and frameworks. • Lead technology transformation initiatives to support business growth and efficiency. • Ensure alignment between global teams, systems, and operational requirements. **Financial \& Vendor Management** • Manage technology budgets, ensuring efficient investment and strong return on spend. • Evaluate and manage third\-party vendors, platforms, and partnerships. • Identify opportunities to optimise costs and improve technology ROI. **Qualifications \& Experience** • Proven experience as a CTO or in a senior technology leadership role within a large\-scale, global organisation. • **Direct experience within the food ordering, hospitality technology, marketplace, or POS/EPOS industry is highly desirable.** • Strong background in building and scaling high\-availability, customer\-facing platforms. • Experience leading large, distributed engineering teams across multiple regions. • Deep technical expertise in modern software architecture, cloud infrastructure, and scalable systems. • Demonstrated success in driving technology transformation and innovation. • Strong understanding of cybersecurity, data protection, and regulatory compliance. • Experience aligning technology strategy with commercial and operational objectives. • Excellent stakeholder management and ability to influence at executive and board level. • Degree in computer science, engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience). **Key Skills \& Attributes** • Strategic thinker with strong technical depth and execution capability. • Innovative and forward\-looking with a passion for technology and product excellence. • Strong leadership and team\-building capabilities. • Highly analytical with strong problem\-solving skills. • Results\-driven with a focus on delivery, quality, and scalability. • Excellent communication skills, able to translate technical concepts to non\-technical stakeholders. • Resilient and adaptable in a fast\-paced, high\-growth environment. • Culturally aware with experience working across global markets. • Willingness and ability to travel internationally on a regular basis. **Benefits** • Competitive compensation and executive benefits package. • Opportunity to lead and scale the technology function of a global platform business. • Direct partnership with CEO and executive leadership team. • Dynamic and collaborative work environment with significant impact and ownership. • 33 days annual leave per year (including public holidays). • Option to buy up to 5 additional annual leave days per year. • Guaranteed day off on your birthday (when requested). • Option to purchase Bupa Select Complete Health Insurance (Medical History Disregarded) for self and family at a significantly discounted rate. • €35 monthly gym contribution.

title: Client Onboarding Analyst II
description: **Work Location** : Dublin, Ireland **Hours** 35 **Line Of Business** TD Securities **Pay Details** We’re committed to providing fair and equitable compensation to all our colleagues. the client onboarding requirements including the new global regulatory framework, credit, legal and AML/KYC, FATCA, CRS, MiFID and understands the role of various stakeholders involved * Working with sales and trading to grow long term relationships with buy side clients as they organically grow with TD * Proactively working with buy side clients to assess needs and priorities daily * Assist the desk and stakeholders in managing priorities * Expediting the ability to trade whilst ensuring all required stakeholders are engaged and risks minimized. * Participate in new client and product strategies for the Dealer globally, offering value\-added ideas and strategies * Work with the Front Office and internal stakeholders daily to understand the client needs and track and prioritize high value relationships * Working with stakeholders to understand regulatory and documentation requirements for large global institutional clients * Building and maintaining strong relationships with internal stakeholders * Maintain client status updates current in the database to track and monitor workflow * Escalate and manage client issues proactively and in a timely manner Job Requirements * Undergraduate degree/ college diploma * Up to 3 years' experience of working experience in either buy side firms or Capital Markets industry * Understanding of Financial Markets and trading products * Proven ability to maintain and manage large client relationships * Demonstrates time management skills and commitment to delivering results within aggressive timelines and ability to support global desks * Ability to analyze, organize and prioritize work while meeting multiple deadlines * Excellent communication and presentation skills to share recommendations with colleagues * High degree of intellectual curiosity, passion, and self\-motivation * Ability to work independently and within a team environment experience * Ability to work within a diverse, global workforce that is client service oriented * Ability to understand and find appropriate applications for data and analysis results * Meticulous attention to detail and due diligence * Sound understanding of risk processes and methodologies * Demonstrates a high aptitude in time management * Design, creativity, and problem\-solving skills * Fast learner who is dedicated to continuously improving their skills and expertise. * Strong OUTLOOK/EXCEL/POWERPOINT skills **Who We Are** TD is one of the world's leading global financial institutions and is the fifth largest bank in North America by branches/stores. Every day, we strive to make every interaction, product, and experience remarkably human and refreshingly simple for over 27 million households and businesses in Canada, the United States and around the world. More than 95,000 TD colleagues bring their skills, talent, and creativity to foster deeper relationships, ensure disciplined execution, and build a simpler, faster banking experience. TD is deeply committed to being a leader in client experience, that is why we believe that all colleagues, no matter where they work, are client facing. Together, we are reimagining what banking can be for our clients, colleagues and communities. **Our Total Rewards Package** Our Total Rewards package reflects the investment we make in our colleagues to help them, and their families achieve their well\-being goals. Total Rewards at TD includes a base salary and several other key plans such as health and well\-being benefits, including medical coverage, paid time off, career development, and reward and recognition programs. **Additional Information** We’re delighted that you’re considering building a career with TD. Through regular development conversations, training programs, and a competitive benefits plan, we’re committed to providing the support our colleagues need to thrive both at work and at home. **Colleague Development** If you’re interested in a specific career path or are looking to build certain skills, we want to help you succeed. You’ll have regular career, development, and performance conversations with your manager, as well as access to an online learning platform and a variety of mentoring programs to help you unlock future opportunities. If you’re passionate about helping clients and building deep, lasting relationships, TD offers diverse career paths where you can grow your expertise and make a meaningful impact. We're committed to your success and foster a respectful workplace where diverse perspectives are valued, everyone has fair opportunities to grow, and you can unlock your full potential to achieve your career goals. Here at TD, we hire and develop the best. **Training \& Onboarding** We will provide training and onboarding sessions to ensure that you’ve got everything you need to succeed in your new role. **Interview Process** We’ll reach out to candidates of interest to schedule an interview. We do our best to communicate outcomes to all applicants by email or phone call. **Accommodation** If you require an accommodation for the recruitment / interview process (including alternate formats of materials, or accessible meeting rooms or other accommodation), please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.

title: Clinical Monitoring Analyst (Cluepoints) - Remote

The CMA contributes to the execution of the Centralized Monitoring process by performing the setup, programming and refresh(es) of the Centralized Monitoring platform, according to the study\-specific Centralized Monitoring Plan (CMP). **Main Responsibilities Include** * Supports the Centralized Monitoring Lead (CML) in the development of the fit\-for purpose CM strategy by contributing to the identification of the relevant CM elements to be implemented for the study (Quality Tolerance Limits (QTL), Key Risk Indicators, data visualizations and Data Quality Assessments) * Setup/Program QTL, KRI and Data Quality Assessments according to the study CMP * Refresh the Centralized Monitoring Platform at predefined frequency to enable signal identification * Generate and maintain the Centralized Monitoring Platform specifications document and file it in eTMF * Review and understanding of protocol is key * Understand the data sources for the study, the data transfer specifications and the data structure * Execute and document peer\-review of CM platform setup done by other CMA colleagues **Experience** * At least 3 years relevant experience in the area of pharmaceutical research and development or related field preferably in centralized Monitoring, data management, and/or biostatistics * 2\-3 years SAS experience including macro language * Knowledge/ experience working on Cluepoints * Ability to work in an autonomous way and engage with relevant stakeholders to execute activities related to study level centralized monitoring process. * Ability to understand basic statistical analysis concepts and to interpret their outcome. * Ability to maintain confidentiality of data and information during interactions with staff at all levels * Ability to work with limited close supervision. * Demonstrated communication, interpersonal, organizational and problem\-solving skills * High levels of initiative, drive and commitment. * Prior experience in a Data Management related field is required * Experience with relational databases, preferably Clinical Data Management and EDC Systems * Experience communicating with all levels of personnel and participating in collaborative work team (locally and globally). * Advanced knowledge of federal regulations, Good Clinical Practice, and Good Clinical Data Management Practices. * Excellent command, both verbal and written, of English **This role is not eligible for UK visa sponsorship** IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare title: Commercial Analyst

title: Commercial Analyst
description: **Position Description** The Flexibility Market Design team is part of the DMSO Design pillar of DMSO (Distribution Markets \& System Operation). The DMSO Design pillar is formerly known as ‘The National Network, Local Connections Programme which was established to deliver the capabilities needed in ESB Networks (people, processes and systems) to change how the electricity distribution system is developed and managed. The Clean Energy Package sets out that “All consumers should be able to benefit from directly participating in the market,” A core objective of the DMSO Design function is to develop integrated designs for ESB Networks’ DMSO organisation. This is an exciting and transformative area, and we are seeking applications from individuals who are interested in playing an active role in establishing these new capabilities in our organisation and our industry. The commercial analyst role is part of the Flexibility Market Design team in the DMSO Design pillar. This team is responsible for setting the development of the appropriate market\-based solutions to enable customers to contribute directly and benefit from the transformation in the distribution network. Through flexible services, customers will be able to offer their capabilities in demand side response, generation or storage to ESB Networks as DSO to efficiently and securely manage the distribution network. The team is responsible for: * Leading the development of flexibility market rules. * Leading the development of new flexibility products in which customers such as demand response, aggregators, generation and storage can participate in via the flexibility market. * Managing existing and future flexibility market contracts such as, but not limited to, validation and settlement of flexibility services. This is done using a Flexibility Services Tracker which will be replaced by a Flexibility Market System over the next 18 months. * Developing appropriate commercial models to value the flexibility products and designing appropriate incentives for customers. * Establishing an appropriate contractual model through which ESB Networks procures and financially settles these flexible services. * Developing the appropriate processes through which customers can participate in transmission services and wholesale markets. **Key Responsibilities** * Engaging in the settlement and validation process for the flexible service providers (customers) in the various flexibility products and services. * Analysis of market data and outcomes to inform flexible service cost forecast * Working with Market Operations Lead in management of the flexible service contracts during commercial operation * Analyse the necessary operational and financial information to prepare performance statements for customers operating within flexible service contracts * Working closely with the Account Holder for each flexible service provider * Involved in developing improvements to the settlement tool and testing of same * Feeding into market process mapping sessions and development of same * Support the development of enduring operational tools such as Flexibility market system for procuring and settling services by contributing to requirements gathering and end user testing * Support the development of the market design and processes (including registration, qualification, competition frameworks and validation and settlement) for participating in flexible services, in collaboration with internal and external stakeholders. * Work closely with other teams to monitor expenditure and support financial projection of flexible service costs. * Supporting development of tender documentation for the individual flexible services and initiatives. **Knowledge, Skills And Experience** Essential * A primary degree in Economics, Finance, or related discipline or equivalent work experience in a commercial role. * 3\+ years relevant experience required * Strong analytical skills * Strong commercial awareness * Good interpersonal skills and ability to work with cross functional teams * Excellent written and communication skills * Excellent Microsoft Excel skills Desirable * Understanding of commercial/contractual risk management * Knowledge of existing Irish or European electricity arrangements. * Knowledge of Procurement rules * Knowledge of probabilistic or statistical modelling techniques * Data analytics capability using Power BI or other data analytics package **Location** Flexible with minimum one anchor day in Finglas. Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration at least two days per week. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business. **Reporting To** Flexibility Market Design Manager, Distribution Markets \& System Operation (DMSO) Design. **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €49,000 to €56,000 per annum **Closing Date** 25th April 2026

title: Commercial Graduate Sales Path - Belgian Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **FY27 Grad Program Job Description \- Sales Path** **Fixed\-term contract** **Duration:** 12 months **Start Date:** August 2026 We are thrilled to invite ambitious graduate candidates who would like to build a career in **Salesforce** to apply for the **5th cohort** of our **EMEA Graduate Rotation Program – Sales Path** . This exciting **12\-month program** is designed to give graduates exposure to both sales and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills for both roles and met our eligibility criteria, top graduates on this path can begin their **Salesforce Sales** careers. The promotion window opens from **month 7 onwards.** Our graduate program is a fully immersive experience, combining comprehensive learning paths with in\-role experience. You will be assigned experienced **Sales Development Buddys** who will provide hands\-on coaching, invite you to join their discovery and qualification calls, giving you the confidence to pick up the phone to reach out to your first salesforce customers. You will also be paired with experienced **Solution Engineer Buddies** during the SE Rotation who will involve you in their live deals. Throughout the programme, you will gain exposure to senior sales and solution engineering professionals during our inspiration sessions. By joining Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundation for a **fast\-paced, successful career** . We are a proud member of the **Fortune 100 Best Places to Work** list for the past 14 years and a consistent winner of **Great Place to Work** awards. Who are we looking for? * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree) from all academic backgrounds * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * You are curious, self\-motivated, and focused on continuous learning * You thrive in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable in a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English \& Dutch What does our EMEA Graduate Program look like? **The Program Is Structured As Follows** Introduction Month During your first month, you will receive extensive onboarding enablement through a Salesforce\-style academy, where you will: * Become immersed in the culture of Salesforce and gain a deep understanding of our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies, and tools * Learn critical professional skills for your career at Salesforce * Follow a structured learning calendar with sessions and hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni Rotations During the Graduate Program, graduates on both the Sales and Solution Engineering Paths will follow the same curriculum and complete the same rotations. Essentially you will gain exposure to both the **Sales and Solution Engineering Roles** . The structure of each rotation is similar: you will spend **60% of your time** immersed in the role with clear tasks, targets, and stakeholder alignment, while the remaining **40%** will focus on role\-specific learning. You will be paired with experienced buddies in each path. If your first rotation (months 2 through 4\) is in **Sales** , you will then join the **Solution Engineering Rotation** from months 5 through 7, and vice versa. Over these six months, you will have built the fundamental skills for these complementary disciplines. Having gone deeper on your solution knowledge, learning how to demo and having exposure to later stages of the sales cycle during the solution engineering rotation will set you up for sucess as you promote throught the various sales roles \- SDR, BDR, AE and beyond. Throughout the rotations, there will be **self\-paced certifications and skills assessments** . You must meet all eligibility criteria before you can **panel for promotion** . Examples of what you can expect during the rotations **Sales Graduate Rotation** * Learn Salesforce’s best\-in\-class prospecting and qualification methodologies * Learn how to use social, digital, video, chat, and phone platforms to effectively engage Salesforce prospects * Collaborate with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation * Learn our solutions at a deeper, more technical level * Work closely with the Solution Engineering organisation, supporting the creation of cutting\-edge customer demonstrations * Learn the art of customer discovery and how to create a compelling customer point of view What’s in it for you? * A clear and structured career path within our Sales organisation * The opportunity to work in an international team within a nurturing environment * Access to our diverse internal employee groups * A comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance throughout your relocation process * An additional seven days of paid time off for charitable causes – learn more about our 1\-1\-1 model Map your future for the coming years With the **EMEA Graduate Program – Sales Path** , you can continue to progress: * After your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong mentoring culture, we strive to develop industry\-leading Solution Engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to build international experience within an organisation that has transformed the enterprise software market through cloud computing. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **FY27 Grad Program Job Description \- Sales Path** **Fixed\-term contract** **Duration:** 12 months **Start Date:** August 2026 We are thrilled to invite ambitious graduate candidates who would like to build a career in **Salesforce** to apply for the **5th cohort** of our **EMEA Graduate Rotation Program – Sales Path** . This exciting **12\-month program** is designed to give graduates exposure to both sales and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills for both roles and met our eligibility criteria, top graduates on this path can begin their **Salesforce Sales** careers. The promotion window opens from **month 7 onwards.** Our graduate program is a fully immersive experience, combining comprehensive learning paths with in\-role experience. You will be assigned experienced **Sales Development Buddys** who will provide hands\-on coaching, invite you to join their discovery and qualification calls, giving you the confidence to pick up the phone to reach out to your first salesforce customers. You will also be paired with experienced **Solution Engineer Buddies** during the SE Rotation who will involve you in their live deals. Throughout the programme, you will gain exposure to senior sales and solution engineering professionals during our inspiration sessions. By joining Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundation for a **fast\-paced, successful career** . We are a proud member of the **Fortune 100 Best Places to Work** list for the past 14 years and a consistent winner of **Great Place to Work** awards. Who are we looking for? * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree) from all academic backgrounds * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * You are curious, self\-motivated, and focused on continuous learning * You thrive in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable in a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English \& Dutch What does our EMEA Graduate Program look like? **The Program Is Structured As Follows** Introduction Month During your first month, you will receive extensive onboarding enablement through a Salesforce\-style academy, where you will: * Become immersed in the culture of Salesforce and gain a deep understanding of our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies, and tools * Learn critical professional skills for your career at Salesforce * Follow a structured learning calendar with sessions and hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni Rotations During the Graduate Program, graduates on both the Sales and Solution Engineering Paths will follow the same curriculum and complete the same rotations. Essentially you will gain exposure to both the **Sales and Solution Engineering Roles** . The structure of each rotation is similar: you will spend **60% of your time** immersed in the role with clear tasks, targets, and stakeholder alignment, while the remaining **40%** will focus on role\-specific learning. You will be paired with experienced buddies in each path. If your first rotation (months 2 through 4\) is in **Sales** , you will then join the **Solution Engineering Rotation** from months 5 through 7, and vice versa. Over these six months, you will have built the fundamental skills for these complementary disciplines. Having gone deeper on your solution knowledge, learning how to demo and having exposure to later stages of the sales cycle during the solution engineering rotation will set you up for sucess as you promote throught the various sales roles \- SDR, BDR, AE and beyond. Throughout the rotations, there will be **self\-paced certifications and skills assessments** . You must meet all eligibility criteria before you can **panel for promotion** . Examples of what you can expect during the rotations **Sales Graduate Rotation** * Learn Salesforce’s best\-in\-class prospecting and qualification methodologies * Learn how to use social, digital, video, chat, and phone platforms to effectively engage Salesforce prospects * Collaborate with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation - Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation * Learn our solutions at a deeper, more technical level * Work closely with the Solution Engineering organisation, supporting the creation of cutting\-edge customer demonstrations * Learn the art of customer discovery and how to create a compelling customer point of view What’s in it for you? * A clear and structured career path within our Sales organisation * The opportunity to work in an international team within a nurturing environment * Access to our diverse internal employee groups * A comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance throughout your relocation process * An additional seven days of paid time off for charitable causes – learn more about our 1\-1\-1 model Map your future for the coming years With the **EMEA Graduate Program – Sales Path** , you can continue to progress: * After your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong mentoring culture, we strive to develop industry\-leading Solution Engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to build international experience within an organisation that has transformed the enterprise software market through cloud computing.
