title: HubSpot Solutions Strategist
description: Salted Stone is a fast\-paced, full\-service digital marketing agency, and we are looking for a **HubSpot Solutions Strategist** to join our growing team. Our mission is to empower growth\-minded companies through creative solutions combined with smart technology. We believe nothing is impossible and always solve for the customer. We aim to attract, engage, and develop exceptional people and, in turn, deliver distinctive, lasting improvements for our customers. This is a mid\-level full\-time position reporting to the Director of Solutions on our Solutions Architecture team (commonly known as Technical Marketing elsewhere). The HubSpot Solutions Strategist will help our clients develop, implement, and optimize new technologies and processes that tie together the entire customer lifecycle using the HubSpot platform. The HubSpot Solutions Strategist supports the solutions team by cultivating professional expertise and applying this to address a range of technical marketing, sales, and service issues. They determine appropriate courses of action based on established guidelines and adapt processes and methods as necessary. Ideal candidates enjoy working directly with our clients and internal team to lead engagements from a technical standpoint (think: ideation up to execution), are organized, great communicators, and aren’t afraid to tackle challenges head\-on! **Job Responsibilities** * Complete and deliver all elements of the Salted Stones Solutions Team Product offerings (Data Migration, Portal Strategy, and HubSpot Onboarding including Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, Service Hub, etc.) * Interact directly with and provide a translation layer to technically\-oriented marketers and software developers, internally and on the client\-side. * Lead client\-facing calls, providing technical expertise and a complete understanding of tools to help clients understand and use offerings effectively. * Run solutions onboarding engagements (Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, and/or Service Hub) without relying on CS team members to run points/project management. * Work closely with the development team (both internally and externally) to connect existing systems to HubSpot. * Be able to generate a standard ERD for either existing or proposed integration structure. * Leverage understanding of data structures to provide recommendations and implementation of systems setup. * Be able to discuss 1st party, 3rd party, and Middleware integration solutions. * Troubleshoot integration and data problems. * Regularly thinks creatively on how to use the HubSpot Platform to solve for client needs. * Develop client\-facing supporting documentation and processes for new technology configurations, customizations, and functionalities. * Execute assigned tasks with a high degree of quality and on time. * Provide accurate time estimates for your work, work within timelines and budgets, and other applicable project management concerns. * Contribute to the continuous improvement of the team by identifying areas for optimization, streamlining processes, and implementing innovative solutions. * Lead pre\-sales engagements from discovery through solution design, translating client business objectives into scalable, technically sound HubSpot strategies and clearly articulating value, approach, and scope to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Deliver tailored solution demonstrations that effectively articulate the value of HubSpot and proposed architectures, aligning platform capabilities to client\-specific requirements, use cases, and desired business outcomes. **Required Competencies** * Successful experience in a Solutions Strategy, Technical Marketing, Marketing/Revenue Technologist, Marketing/Revenue Operations, etc., role (agency experience a plus); ideally suited for early to mid\-career level, but not entry level. * Proven experience with HubSpot (Sales Hub, Marketing Hub, Service Hub). * Excellent organizational skills and the ability to drive and participate in multiple client engagements at once. * Strong technical writing skills \& documentation skills, primarily for creation of compelling client\-facing deliverables. * Ability to confidently communicate with clients as needed. * Excellent project management, organizational and time management skills; and you're comfortable managing multiple projects (budgets, timelines, personalities) simultaneously. * Understanding and/or direct experience with Google Analytics, Databox, and similar analytics tools. * Understanding and/or direct experience with Google Tag Manager. * Basic knowledge of marketing and sales automation tools, business intelligence tools, data enrichment tools, etc. * Strong understanding of sales processes and CRM lifecycle management (pipeline design, lead lifecycle, attribution basics) * Experience contributing to or supporting proposal/RFP responses and statements of work (SOWs). * Ability to lead structured discovery sessions, gathering and synthesizing business, technical, and data requirements. * Demonstrated consultative mindset, with the ability to challenge client assumptions and guide toward better solutions. **S2 General Requirements** * **Team player.** Friendly, authentic, collaborative, and makes an effort with relationships (the S2 team, clients, HubSpot). Shows care and respect for those around them and themselves. Live our Values. * **Growth mindset.** Prioritises learning and self\-improvement. Shows motivation and dedication to growth, personally and professionally. * **Agile** . Adapts to changing priorities and conditions, complexity, and change. * **Proactive** . Demonstrates tenacity and acts without being told what to do. Brings new ideas to S2\. Finds solutions and opportunities, not just problems. * **Prioritizes communication** \- Clear, transparent, and consistent with communication across your team and manager, with other teams, and with clients. If in doubt, over\-communicates. Isn’t afraid to call out and help correct mistakes before they impact the team or the business. * **HubSpot\-centric.** HubSpot is at the center of most work at Salted Stone. Not everyone has to be a HubSpot expert to work here, but everyone needs to have a baseline interest and understanding. **You’ll enjoy working:** In a hybrid role \- our team chose the balance of remote work and contact time in our Dublin office that best suits them and their roles. They’re split between Ireland and Perth, Australia so are used to doing things on Zoom and async. S2’s wider team is spread across North America, Australia, Ireland, and the Philippines so there may be times that we ask you to flex your schedule to allow for collaboration with different timezones. This flex goes both ways. **Benefits** Our Irish team enjoy: * Company paid health insurance and pension match * Birthday and Anniversary Leave (an additional paid day off) * Holisticly wellness benefit * Regular Culture Committee initiatives \& events * Cycle to Work Scheme * Flexible, Hybrid or Remote Work * Team Lunches \& Happy Hours * Company provided laptop **Application process** 1. Resume review 2. Initial call with the Recruitment Team 3. Full interview with the hiring Director \& Regional Director, EMEA 4. Practicum for select applicants 5. Reference checking

title: IAM Operations Engineer
description: **IAM Operations Engineer** The Dell Security \& Resiliency organization (SRO) manages the security risk across all aspects of Dell’s business. You will have an excellent opportunity to influence the security culture at Dell and further develop your career. Join us as an **IAM Operations Engineer** on our **Cybersecurity team** in **Limerick, Dublin or Cork** to do the best work of your career and make a profound social impact. **What you’ll achieve** As an IAM Operations Engineer, you will be responsible for the operational support, optimization, planning, design, testing, and management of Identity \& Access Management (IAM) tools and technologies. You will play a critical part in ensuring secure, reliable identity services across the enterprise by executing daily operational activities, contributing to design discussions, leading troubleshooting efforts, and supporting incident response workflows. Working as part of a global team, you will help to ensure consistent operational execution, maintain strong technical documentation, improve IAM processes, and collaborate with cyber engineering, IT, and business application teams to enhance the organization’s identity security posture. **You will:** * Oversee Incident, Change, Problem, and Knowledge Management processes and troubleshoot IAM tool and process issues such as sync and attribute errors, provisioning failures, authentication issues, and access workflow errors * Create and update detailed documentation (such as runbooks, SOPs, process flows, and knowledge articles) to support daily operations, cybersecurity programs, compliance needs, and audit readiness * Maintain and optimize tools, workflows, processes, reporting, and automation opportunities * Participate in tool and process reviews to identify improvements and evaluate emerging IAM technologies * Support upgrades, patches, migrations, and new integrations by creating and running test plans to ensure IAM capabilities function correctly and meet security requirements **Take the first step towards your dream career** Every Dell Technologies team member brings something unique to the table. Here’s what we are looking for with this role: **Essential Requirements** * 3\+ years of experience in IAM operations, IT operations, cybersecurity, or a related technical field * Hands\-on experience with IAM platforms, such as: + Directory services (Active Directory, Entra ID) + Authentication \& MFA platforms + Identity governance or access management tools * Working knowledge of IAM concepts (JML processes, RBAC, least privilege, access governance, SSO) * Strong technical troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills * Strong communication skills, including English – written and spoken **Desirable Requirements** * Experience supporting or configuring IAM tools such as SailPoint, Okta, CyberArk, Ping, or similar platforms * Familiarity with ITIL processes (Incident, Change, Problem Management) * Experience with security operations workflows or SOC collaboration **Who we are** We believe that each of us has the power to make an impact. That’s why we put our team members at the center of everything we do. If you’re looking for an opportunity to grow your career with some of the best minds and most advanced tech in the industry, we’re looking for you. Dell Technologies is a unique family of businesses that helps individuals and organizations transform how they work, live and play. Join us to build a future that works for everyone because Progress Takes All of Us. **Application closing date: 29th May 2026 \-** Role may close sooner Apply Now! Dell Technologies is committed to the principle equal employment opportunity for all employees and to provide employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. Read the full Equal Employment Opportunity Policy here.

title: IMT Flagship Programme Support
company: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
description: **Company Description** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a postgraduate medical training college dedicated to ensuring that doctors have the skills to provide patients with the best possible care. Founded in 1654, the college supports doctors to enhance their skills, competencies, and professionalism throughout their working lives. Through its Faculties and Institutes, The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, offers medical training programmes, examinations, diplomas, and educational courses to equip doctors with the skills and knowledge they need to care for patients and run safe and efficient healthcare systems. It also offers a range of educational diplomas and courses for learners in the wider healthcare sector. **Job Description** **Role:** IMT Flagship Programme Support **Duration:** 18\-month Fixed Term Contract **Salary:** €47,000 **Reporting to:** Strategic Training Projects Manager **Closing date:** 25 May 2026 at 16:00 All work undertaken within this role will support the overarching principle that patient care remains the priority across all hospital activities and the college is willing and able to support sites to deliver effective training. This role is important for the effective delivery and evaluation of the flagship programme and to ensure no additional pressure is put on BST or local teams with this change. Key Purpose: * Act as the main administrative contact for consultants, trainees, and medical manpower teams involved in the Flagship Internal Medicine Training Programme * Provide administrative support to the management of the Flagship programme * Travel regularly to training sites in Dublin, Galway, and Limerick to build relationships support local programme delivery * Coordinate local reviews local review panels and quarterly meetings * Compile reports of Trainee metrics * Coordinate basic research tasks associated with the programme * Develop strong working relationships with in\-hospital team supporting Trainees * Support the BST operations team * Support the Education Delivery Team in the management of teaching for flagship Trainees Structure of Role * Reporting to Strategic Training Projects Manager * Standing meetings with BST Manager and Team Lead * Provide administrative support directly to programme directors in relation to tasks associated with flag ship programme * This is a hybrid role with an estimate of up to 50% on site activity + This will include same day travel to Limerick and Galway + Candidates should expect at least two days per week in office and this may vary at points in the programme Day to day role * Work as a member of the Internal Medicine Review Project Team * Support the IMT project manager with the delivery of the flagship programme * Lead the coordination, documentation and communication of trainee information * Monitor the frequency and quality of trainee–trainer meetings * Coordinate trainer meetings and programme governance meetings * Maintain an up\-to\-date database of trainees and trainers across the Programme * Support trainee progression and respond to trainee\-related issues or queries. * Coordinate research\-related activities associated with the flagship programme. * Support the organisation of focus groups. * Manage surveys and research data collection. * Liaise with the programme’s research sub\-group. * Support the planning on the 2027 programme intake * Collect and collate programme data for reports and evaluations * Monitor compliance with training standards and accreditation requirements * Assist in organising training events, educational activities, and site visits. * Facilitate communication between training bodies and local sites. * Support initiatives such as educational programmes, examinations, and mentoring activities as required **Requirements** Person Specification * Self\-starter with high motivation, flexibility and the ability to offer a results\-oriented approach with high levels of drive, commitment and enthusiasm * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Excellent stakeholder management skills * Knowledge of project management * Proven track record of achievement in projects administration * Basic research experience * Attention to detail and excellent documentation skills * Ability to work with minimal direction * High personal energy and a positive approach to dealing with challenges, conflict and ambiguity **Competencies:** * Task and Project Management * Problem\-Solving and Result Orientation * Communication and Stakeholder Management * Attention to Detail What We Offer * Flexibility: 35\-hour week hybrid working model. This role will have regular on\-site days in Galway and Limerick, as well as in\-office days. Expect up to 3 in\-person days per week, occasionally less and possibly more at peak points. **Why us?** **Leave:** We offer 21 days annual leave with an additional 3\-4 college days as well as long service leave to all employees. We also provide support for staff with Maternity, Sick, Parents, and Parental leave. **Wellbeing:** An Employee Assistance Programme with Spectrum Life is available to all staff members as well as annual flu vaccine. **Flexible Hybrid working model:** The RCPI offer a flexible 35\-hour\-a\-week hybrid working model for all employees from their start date, giving staff the autonomy to work from home combined with time spent onsite. **Pension:** A defined contribution pension scheme of 1\.5% Employee contribution and RCPI contribution from commencement date with an increase after 18 months **Flexible Benefit:** Pro\-rata €1,500 annual flexible benefit upon completion of a successful probationary period. **Life Assurance:** 4 times your annual salary. **Learning \& Development:** We offer CPD options including study leave and unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning. **Diversity and Inclusion** : RCPI recognises the importance of equality, diversity, and inclusion. We were recently awarded a Silver accreditation from Investors in Diversity. We are an open and inclusive organisation that celebrates and welcomes diversity. We lead initiatives that support, promote and demonstrate a culture of mutual respect, inclusivity, and diversity, as outlined in our Diversity and Inclusion policy. **Accommodations** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is an inclusive employer and in line with our policies you are invited to disclose any reasonable accommodations you require for applying, interviewing or working with RCPI. Please be assured this information will be treated as confidential and not used for the purpose of selection. If you have any queries, please contact Suzann Donnelly at suzanndonnelly@rcpi.ie. **About Us** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a postgraduate medical training college dedicated to ensuring that doctors have the skills to provide patients with the best possible care. Founded in 1654, the college supports doctors to enhance their skills, competencies, and professionalism throughout their working lives. Through its Faculties and Institutes, The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, offers medical training programmes, examinations, diplomas, and educational courses to equip doctors with the skills and knowledge they need to care for patients and run safe and efficient healthcare systems. It also offers a range of educational diplomas and courses for learners in the wider healthcare sector.

title: IT Service Desk Technician
description: Overview: At John Sisk \& Son, we’re more than a construction company, we’re a family\-owned business with a global reach, committed to building excellence in every project. We deliver trust, certainty and value to our clients while embracing innovation and shaping the future of construction. The Service Desk Technician role is responsible for the customer facing and 1st line support role within the Service Desk, on a day\-to\-day basis and to provide support and feedback to the Management \& team coordinator in the running of the department. The role also ensures that incidents and requests are processed within our service level agreements and resolved to customer satisfaction. Responsibilities: * Provide 1st level response to technical software and hardware incidents for all Group users, to agreed service levels both in terms of performance and customer satisfaction. * Liaise with 2nd level technical support staff to ensure support response times are within agreed limits * Installation and configuration of approved hardware, software and mobile devices * Creating, updating and flagging knowledge base articles as required * Clearly communicate technical solutions in a user friendly and professional manner * Keep users informed of call status if requested. * Site attendance as required * Delivers, Tags, sets up and assists in the configuration of end user PC hardware, software and peripherals. * Co\-ordinates timely repair of PC equipment covered by 3rd party vendor maintenance agreements. * Escalation of issues to the Service Desk Management team Experience: * Desktop Operating Systems * Basic hardware for PC’s * Basic Active Directory preferred * Basic Office 365 administration * Service Management system Qualifications: * A\+ certification\-preferred * MCSA/MCITP certification desirable * ITIL Foundation\-preferred Additional Information: * Competitive salary * 26 days annual leave plus additional company days * Pension and benefits * Unlimited learning and development opportunities * Much more **Our People and Recruitment Commitment** At Sisk Group our people are at the heart of our success. We offer the chance to work with purpose, build a career with no limits and be part of a great team. All vacancies are managed directly by our Internal Recruitment Team. Unsolicited contact or speculative CVs from unapproved agencies will not be accepted and no introductory fees will apply. We are an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, disability, age, sexual orientation, religion or any other protected characteristic. Reasonable adjustments are available during the recruitment process. We know the confidence gap and imposter phenomenon can stop talented candidates applying. You do not need to meet every criterion – your skills and potential matter. Don’t hold back we want to hear from you. All recruitment materials comply with legal and regulatory requirements including the EU Pay Transparency Directive and the Equality Acts UK and Ireland. \#LI\-RB1

title: IT Systems Administrator (Security-Focused)
description: IT Systems Administrator (Security\-Focused) Role Overview The IT Operations Systems Administrator is responsible for the design, build, support, and maintenance of complex, high\-quality infrastructure solutions, with a strong focus on Microsoft technologies. This role serves as the primary interface between the Cyber Security Project Team and the IT Operations function, ensuring that security\-driven infrastructure initiatives are effectively designed and implemented. The position plays a key role in delivering strategic security projects, enhancing the organisation's security posture, and ensuring alignment with infrastructure standards, operational requirements, and regulatory expectations. Key Responsibilities Infrastructure \& Security Leadership * Act as Technical Lead for cybersecurity projects delivering infrastructure\-related security initiatives in a critical 24x7 operational environment * Design and implement scalable, resilient, and secure infrastructure solutions aligned with industry best practices * Lead the design and delivery of secure architecture aligned to business, regulatory, and operational requirements Systems Administration \& Operations * Perform daily, weekly, and periodic system administration tasks to maintain system health and performance * Manage vulnerability remediation and security patching across infrastructure environments * Monitor infrastructure and security performance to meet SLA requirements * Support high availability and disaster recovery strategies for critical systems Design \& Implementation * Design and build complex Microsoft\-based infrastructure environments with a focus on security hardening * Implement failover strategies and conduct regular resilience testing * Collaborate with application teams to ensure performance, resilience, and secure traffic flows Support \& Continuous Improvement * Resolve escalated technical issues from first\- and second\-line support teams * Provide ongoing recommendations for process improvements and operational efficiencies * Mentor junior team members and contribute to team capability development Documentation \& Compliance * Produce and maintain high\-quality technical documentation * Support internal and external audit requirements * Ensure compliance with ITIL or similar process control methodologies Knowledge, Skills \& Experience Experience * Minimum 5 years' senior\-level experience working with Microsoft technologies in a large enterprise environment Technical Skills * Strong expertise in Windows Server (2019/2022/2025\) implementation and administration * Advanced knowledge of Active Directory and Group Policy * Solid understanding of network protocols and infrastructure concepts * Experience with backup solutions and principles (NetBackup desirable) * Strong knowledge of Windows 11 client environments Desirable Skills * Experience with VMware or other virtualisation platforms * Knowledge of storage technologies * Linux administration experience (e.g., RedHat, Oracle Enterprise Linux) * Familiarity with automation tools (Python, Ansible, REST APIs) Professional Skills * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities * Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team * Excellent communication skills across all levels of the organisation * Strong organisational, documentation, and administrative capabilities * Experience working in structured environments using ITIL or similar frameworks Qualifications \& Certifications Education * Third\-level qualification in IT or equivalent practical experience Certifications (Required/Preferred) * MCSA, MCP, and/or CCNA * CompTIA Security\+ * Microsoft Certified: Information Security Administrator Associate (with a focus on Microsoft 365\) Additional Information * This role operates within a critical 24x7 environment and may require participation in on\-call or out\-of\-hours support * Strong emphasis on security, resilience, and continuous improvement across infrastructure services

title: IT Systems Administrator | Public Sector
description: **IT Systems Administrator \| Public Sector** At Staffline Recruitment we have a fantastic opportunity available for an experienced IT Systems Administrator to take up a permanent position with a public sector body headquartered in Dublin City Centre but with a national reach. Applicants can be located anywhere within the Republic of Ireland but be able to attend the Dublin City Centre office one day per week. The purpose of this role will be to take responsibility for the execution and support of the organisation's applications and systems operations. This role focuses on backend operational reliability, and the successful candidate will support and maintain multi\-tenant Microsoft 365 tenant operations, including Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, Teams, Intune, Power Platform, Power BI, and related applications. **The role will have the following responsibilities:** * Oversee the management of multiple Microsoft 365 tenants, including Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, Teams, Intune, Power Platform, Power BI, and related applications. * Supervise Active Directory/ADFS on\-premise, Group Policy Management, Azure AD (Entra ID) infrastructure, Exchange on\-prem, and SharePoint on\-prem. * Develop and implement automation strategies using AI, PowerShell and Power Platform, Logic Apps to streamline processes. * Ensure the maintenance of key corporate and program\-related systems. * Oversee device management and onboarding processes using Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune). * Lead the implementation of new solutions, including migrations from on\- * Provide Level 3 technical support for critical applications within the ICT escalation process for end users. * Utilize call logging software to document and resolve user incidents within specified * Diagnose and troubleshoot complex issues using advanced technical expertise, product knowledge, communication, and problem\-solving skills. * Serve as an escalation point for the ICT Service Desk/Support Teams for logged cases/ * Monitor, verify, and maintain daily processes, including logs, alerts, and maintenance * Support the development of knowledge across operational teams through training and * Understand the impacts of change and support teams through transitions by analyzing needs and engaging with relevant stakeholders. * Lead initiatives to improve service delivery and operational * Collaborate closely with different infrastructure and business **Interested applicants will meet the following requirements:** * A relevant third level qualification is desirable. * 5\-10 years of experience in backend systems operations, IT infrastructure, and operational support. * Advanced certifications in Microsoft 365, Entra ID, Active Directory or related technologies are highly desirable. * Excellent communication, report writing, organisational and interpersonal skills. * Strong team leadership and management skills. * Strong stakeholder management skills. * Ability to embrace change by being flexible and enthusiastic to new ideas. This is a permanent position with a salary scale on offer in the region of €70,000 \- €75,000 plus benefits depending on experience. This organisation also offers a flexible hybrid working model with an option to work from home 4 days per week. To be considered for this opportunity, please send an up\-to\-date version of your cv for review to Laura Craughwell at Staffline Recruitment via the enclosed link. **Desired Skills and Experience** Power BI, Microsoft 355, Powershell, scripting, sharepoint, Intune, Power Platform, public sector, non profit, Azure ad, Microsoft Endpoint Manager, OneDrive for Business, Exchange Online, level 3 support, project management, active Directory

title: Infrastructure System Engineer
description: **Snr Microsoft Infrastructure Engineer (M365/ Entra / Azure)** **Job Description:** Our client, a leading organisation, is seeking a Senior Entra / M365 and Azure Engineer to support and enhance their cloud identity and infrastructure environment. title: Intermediary Support Associate
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for almost 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About The Role** An exciting opportunity has arisen within the support function of Davy Select for an Intermediary Support Associate. In the role, the successful candidate will: * Ensure Brokers requirements are understood and met in terms of turnaround and accuracy and that Brokers are consistently provided with excellent customer service; * Manage the opening of new accounts and account queries; * Liaise with internal teams within Davy Operations; * Update broker and client pipelines and Management Information; * Provide general administrative support to the Davy Select Account Managers; * Actively seek to add value to processes by introducing improvements based upon Broker \& Client feedback; * Develop a thorough working knowledge of pension and investments and the appropriate product knowledge; * Involvement in ad hoc projects \& business development as required. * Comply with the Risk and Compliance frameworks, policies and procedures associated with the role. **Requirements** **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: * A Business/Finance degree or equivalent; * QFA qualification or willingness to complete; * Knowledge of settlements and operations; * A proven record in reconciliation and control; * Advanced knowledge of Word, Excel and Outlook; * Good numeric skills; * Customer service focused with a record of providing excellent customer service; * Accuracy and attention to detail; * Excellent organisational skills; * Ability to prioritise and work to deadlines; * A positive attitude, strong work ethic, flexible outlook and a high level of initiative and enthusiasm; **What We Offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. **Reward and Recognition** We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Intermediate PowerApps Developer
description: **INTERMEDIATE POWER PLATFORM DEVELOPER** - Role Start Date: As soon as possible - Accenture Client: Public Sector Client, Dublin, Ireland - Reports To: Accenture/Avanade Power Platform/D365 Technical Lead - Job Type: Full\-time - Working Hours: 8 hours per day, Monday to Friday. This is negotiable depending on personal circumstances. - Experience Required: Around 4 years’ development experience, with 2\.5 years' experience developing on D365 / Microsoft Power Platform **Role Overview:** We’re looking for an **Intermediate Power Platform Developer** to support a major digital‑transformation project modernising legacy systems using Model‑Driven Apps, Dataverse, and D365\. In this role, you’ll build and customise Power Platform solutions, develop workflows with Power Automate, and support data migration from Lotus Notes to Dataverse. You’ll also contribute to integrations across Microsoft 365, Dynamics 365, and third‑party systems. Working closely with the Power Platform/D365 Technical Lead, developers, business analysts, and architects, you’ll help design scalable, secure applications that streamline business processes and follow best‑practice governance. This is a key role in delivering high‑quality solutions within a large public‑sector modernisation programme in Ireland. **Key Responsibilities:** - Design, develop, and implement Power Platform solutions utilising Model\-Driven Apps, Dataverse, Canvas Apps, and related tools. - Create and customise Dataverse entities, views, forms, and relationships to meet business requirements. - Develop and maintain plugins, custom workflows, and PowerApps Component Framework (PCF) controls to enhance functionality. - Integrate and configure WebResources (HTML, JavaScript, CSS) into Power Platform applications for customised user interfaces. - Work closely with business stakeholders to gather and analyse requirements, proposing effective solutions using Power Platform. - Collaborate with functional consultants and project teams to ensure high\-quality solutions are delivered. - Optimise performance and ensure solutions are scalable, maintainable, and aligned with best practices. - Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to Power Platform customisations and integrations. - Ensure compliance with company policies, industry standards, and security protocols in all custom development. **Qualifications:** - Strong experience developing Power Apps, especially Model‑Driven Apps, with a solid understanding of Dataverse architecture and customisation. - Proficient in C\#, JavaScript, and HTML/CSS for plugins, custom workflows, and Web Resources. - Skilled in Power Automate for building workflows and optimising business processes. - Experience with PCF and integrations using APIs, Azure services, and REST. - Excellent problem‑solving and communication skills, comfortable working in fast‑paced, agile, cross‑functional environments. **Preferred Skills:** - Experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 (particularly Field Service or Customer Service modules) and its integration with Power Platform. - Familiarity with Azure DevOps, ALM, and source control processes. - Certification in Power Platform or Dynamics 365 (e.g., PL\-400, PL\-200\) is highly desirable. **What’s in it for you?** At Accenture Ireland, in addition to a competitive base salary, you will also have an extensive benefits package which includes pension, private health insurance, discounted gym membership, emergency caring facilities, and 2 days’ leave per year for charitable work of your choice! Flexible working arrangements can be discussed depending on the position. **Does this appeal to you? If so, we wish to hear from you.** Please advise if you have any particular requirements, so we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at accessIErecruitment@accenture.com Please also note that Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, or gender identity.

title: Intern, Software Engineer
description: **Job Requisition ID \#** 25WD92001 **Position Overview** At Autodesk, we’re reimagining what’s possible. As a global leader in 3D design, engineering, and entertainment software, we help people everywhere imagine, design, and create a better world. Right now, we’re transforming how our products work by moving to the cloud—opening up exciting opportunities for collaboration, innovation, and impact. What sets us apart isn’t just our technology, but our culture. At Autodesk, you’ll find an inclusive, supportive environment where early\-career talent can grow, be creative, and make real impact. It’s one of the reasons we’ve been consistently recognized by **Fortune, Forbes, and Glassdoor as a top place to work** . Over three months in our Summer 2026 Internship Programme, you’ll gain hands\-on experience, mentorship, and opportunities to grow your engineering and domain expertise, while making real contributions to Autodesk’s manufacturing and construction technologies. Alongside technical growth, you’ll develop soft skills and build connections with other interns, locally in your office, regionally across EMEA, and globally, through programs designed just for interns. As a Software Engineer Intern on the Developer Enablement team, you’ll contribute directly to building and scaling applications that utilize environmental data, delivered through a unified standardized interface. You’ll receive mentorship, guidance, and support, while also having the autonomy and responsibility to make a real impact. **Responsibilities** * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to understand the full software development lifecycle * Contribute to real commercial development projects, guided by experienced Autodesk developers * Gain hands\-on experience with TypeScript, AWS, automated testing frameworks, DevOps, and more * Apply Agile and Scrum practices, building your project management skills * Use source control and Continuous Integration platforms such as GitHub and Jenkins * Develop robust APIs to enable sustainability analysis for Autodesk customers * Share your progress and achievements in team meetings, gaining feedback and recognition **Minimum Qualifications** * Currently pursuing a degree in a STEM field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering) * Expected graduation date: please include your month and year of graduation in your application * Experience with API development (REST and GraphQL), through coursework, personal projects, or previous internships * Programming experience (TypeScript preferred) * Enthusiasm for learning new technologies and a proactive approach to problem\-solving **The Ideal Candidate** * Familiarity with automated testing principles to help write reliable, maintainable code * Familiarity with AWS or other cloud platforms, giving you a head start in cloud\-based development * Additional experience with other programming languages or frameworks is a plus * Strong communication skills and ability to work in a team environment * Previous internship experience in a related field is advantageous Learn More **About Autodesk** Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made. We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world. When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us! **Salary transparency** Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience, educational level, and geographic location. **Diversity \& Belonging** We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity\-and\-belonging

title: Investment Analyst - Energy & Infrastructure
description: Our client is seeking a highly motivated Investment Analyst to join a growing Energy \& Infrastructure team. This is an excellent opportunity for an analytically strong finance professional to work across a broad range of transactions within the energy, utilities, infrastructure, and broader real assets sectors. Prior experience within energy or infrastructure is desirable but not essential. **Key Responsibilities:** * Build and maintain complex financial models and valuation analyses * Conduct financial, commercial, and market analysis * Assist with due diligence processes and transaction documentation * Support risk assessment and mitigation analysis across live transactions and investment opportunities * Analyse financial statements, operating metrics, and opportunities * Coordinate with clients, lenders, investors, advisors, and other stakeholders throughout transaction processes * Monitor sector trends and market developments within infrastructure and energy markets **Requirements:** * ACA or CFA qualified (or equivalent) * Approximately 3 years’ experience within investment banking, corporate finance, transaction services, deal advisory or a commercial finance role * Strong financial modelling and analytical skills * Exposure to energy, renewables, utilities, or infrastructure sectors desirable For more information please contact Simon Morris in Morgan McKinley via LinkedIn DM or simonmorris@morgannmckinley.com

title: Investment Associate (Private Equity)
description: **Investment Associate \- Leading Private Equity firm (Dublin)** A leading Private Equity house in Dublin is seeking an Investment professional to join its team. The position will play a key role across the entire transaction process from origination and investment appraisal to deal execution and portfolio management. This is a great opportunity to move to a highly active fund, working with a broad range of business owners, investors and advisers, amongst others. **Responsibilities:** * Assisting in all aspects of the deal process from origination, investment appraisal, through to execution * Conducting market research and company valuations * Supporting deal negotiations with key stakeholders * Ongoing management and assistance of portco's **Ideal Candidate Profile:** • 4\-6 years’ experience in corporate finance, transaction services, deals, investment banking, or capital markets. • Strong understanding of private markets • Proficient in PowerPoint and Excel, with excellent communication and analytical skills. • Self\-motivated, detail\-oriented, and adept at managing multiple projects in a fast\-paced environment. • ACA or CFA essential This is an excellent opportunity to work with a dynamic, fast growing firm. If you would like to find out more please contact Dylan Philpott @ d.philpott@masonalexander.ie

title: Investment Operations (Dealing) - Insurer
description: **Investment Operations (Dealing) \- Life \& Investment Insurer** Our client, a well\-established Life \& Investment Insurance is seeking to appoint an **Investment Operations Analyst** to join their Dealing team, within their Asset Management Division. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with 1–2 years’ experience in investments, fund administration or middle office operations who is looking to develop their career within a Life company environment. **The Role** As part of the Dealing team, you will be responsible for delivering accurate and timely execution of client investment instructions while ensuring strong operational control and risk oversight. Key responsibilities include: * Accurate daily placement and processing of trades * Interpretation of client instructions and preparation of dealing documentation * Instructing settlement of assets and pricing of trades * Processing corporate actions including stock splits, dividends, bonus issues and liquidations * Investigation and reconciliation of cash and unit differences * Preparing reports including outstanding trades and dealing activity * Liaising with brokers, counterparties and internal stakeholders * Monitoring trading activity across multiple markets * Ensuring strict adherence to daily, weekly and monthly deadlines This is a fast\-paced operational role requiring strong attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively. **About You** * 1–2 years’ experience within investments (fund administration, middle office, asset operations or similar) * Strong understanding of trade processing and settlements * Experience with corporate actions is highly advantageous * Degree in Business, Finance or related discipline (minimum 2\.1 preferred) * Strong Excel skills (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP, data analysis) * High attention to detail and strong analytical skills * Ability to work both independently and as part of a collaborative team If you’re looking to move into a structured Life company environment and build depth in Investment Operations, this role offers excellent exposure and career progression. **For a confidential discussion, contact Honor Moloney.** honor.moloney@abrivia.ie All applications will be treated with the strictest confidence.

title: Jewellery Polisher - Maternity Leave Contract
description: Bulgari is one of the most prestigious brands and global players on the luxury market. We design, manufacture, distribute and sell high\-end jewels, watches, perfumes and accessories through a network of over 250 owned stores in the most exclusive shopping areas in the world and selected distributors and more than 10\.000 exclusive perfumeries. Quality and excellence are the basis of our culture that reflects the same commitment within the working environment to achieve an organisation aimed at the full satisfaction of the customer. Excellence for Bulgari means the perfect balance between top quality products and impeccable services worldwide. Bulgari belongs to the LVMH Group (Louis Vuitton Moët Hennessy group), the global leader in the luxury sector. **Candidates must be eligible to work 40 hours per week with no restrictions.** **Working hours: shift starts at 7 AM Irish time, Monday to Friday** **Job Responsibilities:** * Perform the polishing phase, based on technical documentation, working cycles and procedures * Perform all technical activities related to polishing up to final phase respecting high quality standards * Properly use all technical or manual tools supporting the polishing phases. Respecting specific material properties such as gemstones, diamonds, gold, silver, platinum, stainless steel and alternative materials * Perform specific self\-quality control accordingly to internal quality control procedures * Properly use supporting machines such as polishing machine, ultrasonic washing machine, rhodium plating, laser welder, laser engraving * Guarantee productivity target assigned (quantity, lead times, and quality). Adaptability and flexibility to accommodate business needs. * Waste management of materials according to procedures to support precious material recovery Implement the Lean principles to maintain and improve order in working place (5S\-Lean) * Respect security and health and safety rules and procedures * Maintain inventory activities * Support business projects related to rework activities * Support company training programs * International Travel up to approx. 10% annually is required for this position **Requirements:** * Beginners’ goldsmith experience/training * Knowledge of product technical specifications, handling tools and techniques of goldsmithing and polishing * Knowledge of materials’ chemical properties applied to the specific phase * Knowledge of safety processes \& legislation * Understanding of all guidelines, procedures and regulations by international institutions and agreements (e.g. RJC, CoC) * Experience with MS Office Suite, WMS systems required, SAP desirable * Experience with Power BI is desirable * 3rd level education required **Interpersonal skills:** * Works proactively within a team environment * Adaptability and problem\-solving * Communication and organisationalskills * Integrity, honesty, and professionalism * Creativity and attention to detail * Team player * Leadership
