manage departmental and research budgets effectively. * Participate in training and professional development programs as needed **Knowledge \& Experience – (Essential):** * Outstanding organisational and administrative abilities, with a keen eye for detail and a high level of accuracy. * Proven experience in providing high\-quality administrative and/or secretarial support at management level. * Strong ability to work independently, using initiative, as well as collaboratively within a team environment. * Excellent task and time management skills, with a demonstrated ability to plan, prioritise, and deliver work to meet deadlines and achieve objectives. * Advanced IT proficiency, particularly in Microsoft Office applications including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Teams, OneDrive, and SharePoint. * A proactive, solution\-focused approach with a ‘can\-do’ attitude and a strong commitment to team success and continuous improvement. * Self\-motivated with the ability to identify challenges and implement effective solutions. * Demonstrates discretion and sensitivity in handling confidential information. * Quick to learn and responsive to evolving needs and priorities; takes initiative in contributing ideas and improvements. * Be responsive and supportive to the needs of the teaching and research teams * Demonstrate strong working relationships with team\-members and more broadly across the institution * Receptive to guidance and direction, with a strong ability to deliver results under pressure and meet deadlines **Desirable Skills** * Experience or enthusiasm to engage with and manage social media accounts and content * Familiarity with software/platforms relevant to the role, e.g., RISR (assessment management), Agresso (finance), RIMS (research management information system) or similar. *We are all too aware that imposter syndrome and the confidence gap can sometimes stop fantastic candidates putting themselves forward, so please do submit an title: People & Culture Advisor
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **People and Culture** **at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our team has created a workplace of inspiration and growth. Be a part of building an inclusive and empowering culture, where every colleague’s unique abilities are recognised and celebrated. Collaborate with a team committed to the wellbeing of our workforce and define what it means to work at Primark. **What You’ll Do as** **People \& Culture Advisor** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Build strong, trusted relationships with business stakeholders, providing tailored P\&C advice, coaching leaders on best practice, and offering strategic guidance on complex people matters. * Manage employee relations cases (performance, misconduct, grievances), ensuring fair and legally compliant outcomes while coaching managers to effectively lead and develop their teams. * Partner with Talent Acquisition and hiring managers to deliver a proactive recruitment strategy, support interviews, and ensure a positive, professional candidate experience. * Oversee and support the full employee lifecycle—from onboarding to performance management, engagement, employee relations, and offboarding—continuously improving processes and colleague experience. * Use P\&C metrics, analytics, and engagement survey insights to identify trends, inform people plans, and design targeted interventions, training, and action plans with business leaders. * Lead and support talent management, succession planning, and P\&C initiatives, collaborating with COEs and People Services to implement best practices, policies, and annual P\&C priorities aligned to business needs. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Minimum 3\+ years’ HR experience, including coaching and partnering with line managers, with strong exposure to employee relations, performance management, and talent management. * Degree in HR or a related discipline preferred, with sound judgement, strong ethics, and the ability to manage highly confidential and sensitive matters with integrity. * Proven ability to build effective working relationships across all organisational levels; approachable, empathetic, and emotionally intelligent with strong communication skills. * Highly organised and detail\-oriented, able to prioritise, multitask, manage demanding workloads, analyse data, and meet deadlines in a fast\-paced, agile environment. * Resilient, flexible, and solution\-focused, demonstrating initiative, accountability, and adaptability in continuously changing business environments. * Strong influencing, coaching, and presentation skills, capable of translating complex information into clear, actionable insights and leading others through change. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-8483

title: Pharmacy Dispenser
description: **The importance of your role.** This is your chance to be part of something a bit different. You’ll help our customers feel good. Giving them fantastic customer care and that’ll make you feel good – and we think that’s a great opportunity. Making our customers feel better is what it’s all about. It’s everything from dispensing what they need, to your gentle advice that’ll put them at ease and you’ll let them know that you’re there for them. Together these elements will play a big part in making us No 1 for Customer Care. **What You’ll Be Doing Day To Day.** As a Dispenser your customer care will be really wonderful. It’s more than providing the medicine customers need it’s giving them a reassuring experience as a customer. It’s the little things that make a big difference, like a friendly chat. Using your expert knowledge and brilliant advice you’ll put our customers at ease. We’ve so many fantastic health services on offer, why not let our customers know about how they can benefit from them, by recommending how we can support them. It could be a small tip that makes all the difference. You’ll support the Pharmacy team, as the first point of call for customer queries. Inspire them with your amazing customer care and attention to detail. Your love for fantastic, and really personable service will keep our customers coming back. You’ll be part of making them feel good on so many levels. **How will you do it?** Build great relationships with the Pharmacy team, the more advice we can give the better our customers feel. Share what you know with your team, it could make such a difference to someone. Know our customers – it can make such a difference to the way we do things. By passing on feedback and taking action, you’ll be part of improving our care, even more. We’re so proud of the standards we have, and we want to look after our customers, so getting what they need is really important to us. **What You’ll Need To Have.** We’re looking for really great people, with a friendly personality. It’s all about working together as one great team – after all it’s our people that are at the heart of our business. It’s a fantastic opportunity to add to your own development, and a chance to learn more about us as a business. It could open up even more opportunities with us. You’ll need to have lots of professional and Pharmacy experience, along with really wonderful customer service skills. But it isn’t just about experience, we’re interested in seeing a bit more, the real you – and that makes us all feel good. **Please note, any salary estimates given on third\-party sites are not provided or endorsed by Boots Ireland and may not be accurate.**

title: Procurement Associate
description: A top\-tier global investment manager is hiring a Procurement Associate to join its growing European office in Dublin. This is a hands\-on commercial role for a proven procurement professional who wants broad category exposure, senior stakeholder visibility and the chance to operate inside one of the most respected investment platforms in the market. **WHY THIS ROLE STANDS OUT** 🟣 Join a global investment manager with a best\-in\-class reputation and a growing European presence 🔵 High\-profile role working across IT, Legal and Finance at senior level 🟣 Own the full procurement cycle \- sourcing, negotiation and contract management 🔵 Strong compensation and genuine scope to improve processes, controls and automation 🟣 Fast\-paced, meritocratic culture where top performers are recognised **THE ROLE** 🟣 Lead end\-to\-end procurement across multiple categories including IT 🔵 Run RFx processes, evaluate vendors and drive selection decisions 🟣 Review, draft and manage contracts \- negotiating pricing, terms and commercial conditions 🔵 Partner with IT, Legal and Finance to align procurement with business needs 🟣 Monitor supplier performance and maintain key vendor relationships **CANDIDATE PROFILE** 🟣 5\-10 years' procurement, sourcing or supply chain experience \- IT sourcing a plus 🔵 Strong command of contract management and commercial terms 🟣 Sharp negotiator with a structured, analytical approach 🔵 Advanced Excel; experience with GenAI procurement tools advantageous

title: Procurement Executive
description: Carr Golf Maintenance is currently looking to recruit a **Procurement Executive,** on a fixed term contract, initially to cover a period of maternity leave for 1 year from mid June. This role is based in our HQ at Parkwest Business Park, Dublin 12 however, infrequent national travel will be required once a quarter. Carr Golf Maintenance, part of the Carr Golf Group, is a trusted partner to golf club owners, managers, Committees and green keepers. Our customised course maintenance solutions provide continuous, never\-ending improvement through environmentally friendly and sustainable agronomic leadership, rigorous testing and measuring, improving productivity, maximising efficiency and digitalisation of maintenance operations, which heightens customer satisfaction, bolstering the reputation and appeal of their courses. The company has been a leader in golf services in Ireland for over 35 years. The Procurement Executive will be responsible for cost\-effectively purchasing inputs and supplies for over 25 golf course maintenance operations based in Ireland \& UK managing supplier relationships, working closely with the finance department on budget controls and reconciliations **Job Summary** The Procurement Executive oversees sourcing to optimize costs, manage risks, and ensure a seamless supply chain for each golf course. You will oversee the end\-to\-end purchasing process, from requisition to payment, often supporting multiple business units across the company. **Key Responsibilities** * **Strategic Sourcing \& Negotiation:** Work with the team to implement sourcing strategies for goods/services, negotiating with suppliers for the best price, quality, and delivery terms. * **Supplier Relationship Management (SRM):** Build and maintain relationships with vendors, evaluating their performance against quality and reliability standards. * **Budgeting \& Cost Control:** Monitor spending to ensure alignment with budget guidelines, targeting cost\-saving opportunities and reducing operational expenditures. * **Inventory \& Logistics:** Monitor stock levels of operational items, to include quarterly stock takes at client courses, while managing and overseeing general stock at HQ and stock transfers to sites. * **Compliance \& Risk Mitigation:** Ensure compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations, and arrange training for team members. * **Technology Utilisation:** Utilise procurement management software to streamline processes and analyse purchasing data. * **Team Leadership:** Work closely with Regional Superintendents, Plant \& Machinery Manager and Head Greenkeepers to ensure efficient supply of goods. **Requirements and Qualifications** * **Education:** Relevant qualification in procurement or related field. * **Experience:** Proven experience in a procurement role or department. * **Skills:** + **Negotiation \& Interpersonal Skills:** Ability to build partnerships and secure advantageous terms. + **Analytical Ability:** Strong data analysis skills to evaluate supplier performance and market trends. + **Leadership:** Experience in managing and mentoring team members. + **Technology:** Experience using IT systems to manage the procurement process, budget control and financial reporting * **Knowledge:** Understanding of the golf industry would be an advantage **Benefits** * Competitive Salary * Hybrid working arrangements – Tuesday to Thursday are office based with Monday and Friday remote working. * Twenty\-Five Days Annual Leave * Golf Membership at Company Operated Golf Clubs * Discounted Membership Rates for Friends and Family * Ongoing Professional Development Support

title: Product Owner
description: We’re looking for an experienced Product Owner with a passion for delivering value who will work with our teams to deliver and configure products for our extensive range of partners, while also evolving the core services and system capabilities that underpin our partner ecosystem. This role will play a key part in driving scalable, reliable and high\-performing solutions across complex integrations and internal workflows. This is an outstanding opportunity for an analytical problem solver who enjoys working across interconnected systems, translating strategic objectives into structured roadmaps and high\-quality delivery artefacts. **Your Journey starts here!** **What you’ll do as a Product Owner:** * Maximise the value of our products by delivering high\-quality integrations and enhancements across core services, optimising scalability, reliability and performance across partner\-facing and internal systems. * Own and maintain a clear, prioritised roadmap within your domain, balancing strategic objectives, technical dependencies and operational requirements to drive predictable, high\-quality delivery. * Clearly articulate and manage requirements, working with stakeholders and the delivery team to break down, outline and refine user stories and acceptance criteria. Have a clear focus on always delivering business value * Take ownership for successful delivery and launch of solutions, collaborating effectively across teams to manage dependencies and ensure successful outcomes * Work closely with Product Managers to understand business and commercial context, priority and support definition of solutions * Work closely with our Go\-Live, Partner Management, Trading, Supply, Data Science and Finance teams to understand technology and business requirements across internal and external systems. * Collaborate with Product Managers and Engineering Managers to understand the capabilities of our products, APIs and services, and the options for scaling and configuring them effectively. * Producing high quality artefacts related to business rules, requirements, features etc to ensure that information is readily available and clearly communicated to the wider organisation. * Identify opportunities for product improvements and support product managers in shaping product roadmap initiatives * Maximise the value of our products by delivering best\-in\-class integrations, optimising scalability and driving performance of products for existing partners **What you'll bring to the team** * 3 years' experience as a Product Owner in an agile environment, ideally delivering web and/or API\-driven products within an e\-commerce context. * Comfortable engaging in discussions around APIs, system behaviour, dependencies and performance to enable effective collaboration with engineering teams. * Agile / Scrum certification (e.g. CSPO, PSPO) is desirable. * Third\-level qualification in Business, Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, or a related discipline OR equivalent practical experience in a product, technology, or delivery r * oleStrong understanding of lean/agile principles and the full software delivery lifecycle * Experience working on backend or service\-oriented products, including integrations, data flows or operational systems, is an advantage. * Proven track record of collaborating effectively with engineers, designers, and senior leadership to drive product outcomes * Strong backlog management experience: refinement, prioritisation, dependency management, release planning * Comfortable using data and KPIs to prioritise work and measure impact (e.g., conversion, performance, quality) * Demonstrated ability to apply both creative and analytical thinking to complex problem\-solving * Experience working across multiple devices and channels to deliver cohesive customer experiences

title: Programme Supervisor
description: #### **Job description** We are an independent voluntary organisation providing services and support for people with disabilities and their families for more than 70 years. Our work positively impacts the lives of over 12,000 people with disabilities, equipping them with the confidence, skills, and self\-belief to achieve their expressed goals **We Welcome All Backgrounds and Abilities!** We are an inclusive employer and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds, including those with different abilities. We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible work environment for all individuals. If you require any reasonable accommodations during the application process or once employed, please let us know. Examples of accommodations we can provide include modified workstations, flexible work hours, assistive technologies, or adjustments to communication methods during interviews or in the workplace. Our goal is to ensure that all candidates and employees have the support they need to succeed in their roles. Please contact us at any stage. We will work with you to meet your needs. **Join Our Team and Make a Difference!** **Programme Supervisor\- Ref: 17029** **Specific Purpose Part Time (39 Hours) Contract** **Location:** **About the Role:** The purpose of the job is to promote and support independent living and community integration personal development and work opportunities for service users. The role will be based across two resource centres. To supervise staff and to assist the Manager in the development, implementation and delivery of quality customer focused and cost effective community services to service users at an appropriate time and setting. This includes the provision of individual programme’s and activities to meet the needs, choices and abilities of service users in line with the HSE New Directions policy and guidelines. The role may require travel across locations most frequent in Co. Wicklow and Dublin city. **What we’re looking for:** * A Third level graduate, health and social care equivalent * MAPA, Autism Awareness Training, Occupational First Aid, Manual Handling BA in a relevant field * Minimum two years’ experience of working with adults with disabilities * Experience of the design and delivery of individual programmes for people with disabilities or those who are marginalized **Your Responsibilities** * Build trusting relationships and communication with service users, facilitating them to identify their goals, strengths and needs. Support service users to establish and enhance their natural support circle. * Supporting service users to identify the community based supports they would like to avail of and work with service users to identify practical solutions to any barriers to accessing these supports. * Provide service users with accessible information on available community based supports, ensuring all activities and supports are monitor and are maintained at an appropriate pace to ensure sustainability. Submit your application by 25/05/2026 **Salary starting from point 1 € 39,120\.94 to point 5 € 45,325\.68 per annum ( pro\-rata) , is subject to negotiation depending on experience and qualification.** **What we Offer:** * Company Pension Scheme * Paid maternity/paternity/adoption leave * Educational leave * 27 days annual leave * Bike to work scheme * Tax saver travel scheme * Income protection * Unpaid leave * Health and Wellbeing programme * Company credit union scheme * Employee discount scheme * Digital Doctor * Financial support for studying and study leave * Long Service Reward Scheme * Sick leave * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) To view the full extensive job description please, click here Job Description These duties and responsibilities are a reflection of the present service requirements and may be subject to review and amendment to meet the changing needs of the services. **The Rehab Group is an equal\-opportunity employer**

title: Project Coordinator
description: Project Coordinator **Role Details** * Location: Dublin 16 (Onsite Parking Available) * Hybrid, Onsite 3 days per week * Contract: Permanent **About the Role** We are seeking a highly organised and detail\-oriented Junior Project \& Programme Coordinator to support the delivery, coordination, and continuous improvement of a range of academic and operational projects across the organisation. This role would suit someone with strong administrative and project coordination skills who enjoys managing multiple priorities, following processes through to completion, and working collaboratively across teams. The successful candidate will play a key role in coordinating schedules, maintaining accurate documentation and records, supporting reporting processes, liaising with stakeholders, and ensuring projects and reviews are delivered efficiently and on time. **Key Responsibilities** * Coordinate and support a variety of projects, review cycles, and operational activities * Manage timelines, schedules, documentation, and action tracking * Maintain accurate records, templates, and reporting systems * Support the preparation of reports, presentations, and project updates * Liaise with internal teams and external stakeholders to ensure smooth delivery of activities * Monitor progress across multiple workstreams and follow up on outstanding actions * Assist with data management, reporting, and process improvement initiatives * Support meetings, governance activities, and review processes, including minute taking and follow\-up actions * Contribute to continuous improvement and operational efficiency across the organisation * Provide general administrative and project support as required **Candidate Profile** * Exceptionally organised with strong attention to detail * Comfortable managing multiple priorities and deadlines simultaneously * Proactive, reliable, and able to follow tasks through to completion * A strong communicator with excellent interpersonal skills * Confident working with data, systems, and Microsoft Office tools, particularly Excel * Able to build positive working relationships across teams * Interested in project coordination, programme management, operations, or process improvement

title: Project Coordinator - Pharmaceutical Manufacturing
description: We are partnering with a leading global life sciences organisation to recruit a Project Coordinator to support a major pharmaceutical manufacturing expansion project. This is an exciting opportunity to play a key role in coordinating operational readiness activities within a large\-scale, high\-impact manufacturing environment. This role is ideally suited to someone looking to develop their career along the Junior Project Manager to Project Manager pathway. The Project Coordinator – Operational Readiness supports the planning, coordination and execution of cross\-functional readiness activities across key project and manufacturing milestones. The role acts as a central coordination point between Manufacturing, Quality, Engineering, EHS and other stakeholders to ensure operational readiness activities remain aligned and on schedule. The successful candidate will monitor progress, manage dependencies and support risk escalation to enable safe, compliant and efficient start\-up operations. This position suits a highly organized individual with strong communication and project coordination skills within a manufacturing or project\-based environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Coordinate operational readiness activities across major project and manufacturing milestones. * Track schedules, risks, dependencies and action items across cross\-functional teams. * Support integrated planning, readiness reviews and stakeholder alignment activities. * Monitor readiness metrics including training, documentation and milestone status. * Escalate issues or delays impacting critical manufacturing or validation timelines. **Key Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in Science, Engineering or a related discipline. * 3\-5 years experience in project coordination within pharma or biotech. * Understanding of commissioning, qualification and operational readiness activities. * Experience using MS Office, reporting tools and dashboards for project tracking. * Strong organizational, communication and stakeholder management skills. This is a 12 month contract, based 3 days onsite in Dun Laoghaire. Due to the high volume of applications, we may not be able to respond to every applicant. AscendPM do not forward candidate CVs to clients without prior consent.

title: Qualified Early Years Educator
description: Little Rainbows is a very friendly family run childcare service. We aim to create a home from home environment for our children and families. Here at Little Rainbows we understand that our staff are a huge asset to our creche and aim to create a supportive and happy working environment for our team. **EXCITING OPPORTUNITY:** Little Rainbows are currently looking for a kind, caring and dedicated person to come work in our purpose built state of the art early childhood education and care facility. **Positions:** We require someone with a childcare qualification to work a 4 day or 5 day week in our junior Toddler room and wobbler room. **The person we are looking for:** * We are looking for an individual with good communication skills and a warm and caring personality. * Someone who is dedicated to their role as a childcare practitioner and will build positive rapport with other staff, children and parents. * Somebody with a “can\-do” attitude and to be aware of the importance of teamwork within a creche setting. * A very low staff turnover. **Applicants must have:** * A recognised childcare qualification (minimum level 5 in childcare only) * Experience preferred or a strong willingness to learn * Must be willing to be Garda Vetted * Staff who have lived outside of Ireland for more than six months must provide translated Police Vetting **What Little Rainbows Offer:** A supportive, approachable management team Competitive Salary (based on experience and qualifications) Career Progression On\-going Training Social Events Staff of the month bonus Free on\-site parking Free Lunch treats throughout the year Flexible hours A friendly, supportive work environment . Work remotely * No Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €15\.50\-€17\.00 per hour Benefits: * Company events * On\-site parking * Sick pay Application question(s): * Do you have a recognised childcare qualification and live close to the creche Education: * Advanced/Higher Certificate (preferred) Language: * English (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: RGI Plumber/Supervisor
description: Senior RGI Qualified Plumber/Supervisor \- Permanent Position and join our growing team in Dublin. **Due to growth, we are looking to add more plumbers to our existing team.** We're seeking a skilled and ambitious **RGI Qualified Plumber/Supervisor** to join our established installation and maintenance team. This is an excellent opportunity for a seasoned professional who values work\-life balance, career progression, and working with a company that invests in its people. What We Offer You ✅ **Work\-Life Balance**: Monday\-Thursday 8am\-4\.30pm, Friday 8am\-3\.30pm ✅ **Financial Rewards**: Competitive salary \+ quarterly performance bonuses ✅ **Career Growth**: Direct reporting to Technical Director with development opportunities ✅ **Job Security**: Permanent contract with an established, growing company ✅ **Professional Environment**: quality standards, ongoing training The Role As a **Senior RGI Technician/Supervisor**, you'll be the technical expert our clients rely on. Working both independently and as part of our close\-knit team, you'll: **Core Responsibilities:** * Lead gas boiler installations and comprehensive servicing programs * Diagnose and resolve complex heating system issues using your expertise * Mentor junior team members and share your knowledge * Maintain detailed work documentation and provide technical recommendations * Ensure all installations meet regulatory standards and manufacturer specifications * Plan efficient work schedules to maximize productivity and client satisfaction **What Makes This Role Special:** * Variety in your day \- from challenging problem\-solving to rewarding installations * Direct client interaction where your expertise is valued and respected * Opportunity to work with the latest heating technology and equipment * Input into company processes and technical decisions What You Bring **Essential Requirements:** * **QQI Level 6 Advanced Certificate** in Plumbing (or equivalent qualification) * **Minimum 3\+ years** of hands\-on experience in gas heating systems * **Current RGI Registration** with strong technical competency * **Full clean Irish driving license** **The Ideal Candidate:** * **Technical Excellence**: Deep knowledge of modern gas boilers, heating controls, and system diagnostics * **Customer Focus**: Natural ability to explain technical issues clearly and build lasting client relationships * **Problem\-Solver**: Takes ownership of challenges and finds practical solutions * **Professional Pride**: Committed to quality workmanship and continuous improvement * **Team Player**: Collaborative approach while comfortable working independently Why Choose Us? We're not just another employer \- we're a company that understands the value of experienced tradespeople. Our team members enjoy: * **Respect for your expertise** and input into technical decisions * **Stable client base** with ongoing maintenance contracts * **Investment in your development** with training on latest technologies * **Professional working environment** with proper tools and support * **Recognition and rewards** through our quarterly bonus system Ready to Take the Next Step? If you're a skilled RGI professional looking for a role that offers both challenge and stability, we'd love to hear from you. **Apply today** to join a team that values experience, professionalism, and quality work. *All applications will be treated in strict confidence.* Pay: €60,000\.00\-€65,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company car * Company pension * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Receptionist
description: **Job Description** **Location:** Windward Management Limited, 19/20 Fleet Street Join our fabulous team at The Fleet Hotel as a **Front Office** **Receptionist** . **About Us** The Fleet Hotel is a wonderful boutique style Hotel located in the centre of Dublin on the edge of Temple Bar. It comprises of some beautiful period buildings including the iconic Bewleys Café, much loved by Dubliner’s for decades. The Fleet Hotel is synonymous with local Dublin history, before becoming the Fleet Hotel, 19/20 Fleet Street was home to Bewley’s Café which was established by Ernest Bewley in 1905\. Bewleys were known to be one of the finest Tea Houses in Europe and a favourite with Dubliner’s for generations. **Job Summary** The receptionist is responsible for providing administrative support across the organization. This role involves welcoming guests and clients, managing front desk operations, handling phone calls, and performing various administrative tasks to ensure efficient daily operations. **Key Responsibilities** Front Desk Management: Greet and welcome visitors in a warm and friendly manner. Answer, screen, and forward incoming phone calls. Ensure the reception area is tidy and presentable, with all necessary stationery and materials (e.g., pens, forms, brochures). **Administrative Support** Receive and sort daily mail and deliveries. Maintain office security by following safety procedures and controlling access via the reception desk (monitor logbook, issue visitor badges). Assist with scheduling and coordinating meetings and appointments. Manage office supplies inventory and place orders when necessary. Perform other clerical duties such as filing, photocopying, transcribing, and faxing. **Customer Service** Address and resolve basic inquiries and concerns from visitors and clients. Provide accurate information about the organization and its services. Complete check in/out protocol **Communication** Communicate with other departments to ensure smooth operations. Update calendars and schedule meetings as needed. **Requirements** The ideal candidate will possess at least 1 years’ experience as a Receptionist in a 4\* hotel setting Hotsoft experience desirable **About The Role** **Perks and Benefits of working at The Fleet Hotel** * Competitive rate of pay * PRSA pension scheme * Career progression opportunities across the Windward group * Employee Assistance Programme * Digital Wellness Programme * Complimentary, meals on duty including breakfast, lunch \& dinner * Discounted Stays \& Dining for Friends and Family Enjoy exclusive reduced rates on accommodation and dining across all Windward Hotels, with both city and resort destinations available for your loved ones. * Employee Rewards \& Recognition Be part of a workplace that values your contribution, with recognition initiatives such as Employee of the Quarter awards, appreciation events, and occasional surprise treats to celebrate our team’s achievements. **About Windward Management** The Fleet Hotel is managed by Windward Management. Windward Management is a team of highly experienced hotel operators and investors with a hotel portfolio of approx. 2,000 keys\* in Ireland and Continental Europe. Founded in 2007, Windward is a leading hospitality management company, managing and operating hotels on behalf of their owners. Join Us Today as a Receptionist! **Skills Needed** **About The Company** Since its inception in 2007, Windward Management Limited has built a reputation for excellence in hotel management. With headquarters in Dublin, the company provides tailored solutions for hotel owners, including operations, marketing, and financial management. Windward's team is driven by a passion for delivering exceptional guest experiences while ensuring each property achieves its full potential. Recognized for its dedication to innovation and operational success, the company is equally committed to investing in its people. Professionals joining Windward Management will become part of a supportive and dynamic environment, with abundant opportunities to grow and excel within a thriving sector. **Company Culture** At Windward Management, people are at the heart of success. The company nurtures a culture rooted in respect, collaboration, and a shared commitment to excellence. Employees are empowered to innovate and contribute to meaningful outcomes while enjoying a strong focus on work\-life balance. Windward fosters professional growth through tailored development opportunities, ensuring each team member can achieve their career aspirations. This positive, inclusive workplace values individual contributions, encourages open communication, and celebrates success collectively. For hospitality professionals seeking a rewarding career in an environment where their efforts truly matter, Windward Management offers an exceptional opportunity to thrive. **Required Criteria** **Desired Criteria** **Closing Date** Saturday 6th June, 2026 **Contract Type** parttime **Salary** Based on Experience

title: Recruiter
description: We are looking for an experienced Recruiter to support Sidetrade's continued growth across Sales, Customer Success, Product, Engineering, and Corporate functions. You will play a critical end\-to\-end recruitment role, partnering closely with leaders to attract and hire top\-tier talent in a competitive SaaS and AI market. **About Sidetrade** Sidetrade is an AI company, listed on Euronext Growth, on a mission to revolutionize the way enterprises unlock value from their customers leveraging its Order\-to\-Cash Intelligence platform and its Data Lake. We're proud of our 38 nationalities and these diverse perspectives drive our innovation, one team culture and a customer\-first mindset. Sudetrade is positioned as a **Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ Leader** since 2022\. We value passion over perfection. So, if you're eager to learn and bring great energy, we want to hear from you. Be you. Grow with us. Curious about Sidetrade? Catch the **Sidetrade Inside Out podcast** . **Requirements** **What you'll be doing** : * Manage the full recruitment lifecycle, from workforce planning and intake meetings to offer negotiation and onboarding handover * Hire across multiple roles including Go\-To\-Market, Tech, Product, Data, and G\&A * Develop a deep understanding of Sidetrade's business, culture, and growth roadmap * Act as a trusted talent advisor to hiring managers and leadership * Challenge and refine hiring needs, role scopes, and profiles based on market insight * Provide regular updates, pipeline reporting, and hiring recommendations * Build proactive pipelines using direct sourcing, LinkedIn, referrals, and talent mapping * Attract passive candidates in highly competitive SaaS / AI markets * Conduct market benchmarking and advise on talent availability, compensation, and hiring strategies * Deliver a world‑class candidate experience aligned with Sidetrade's brand and values * Represent Sidetrade professionally with senior and executive\-level candidates * Partner with Communication team to support recruitment campaigns * Use ATS and recruitment metrics to drive continuous improvement * Ensure recruitment processes are compliant with local employment regulations **What you will bring** : * Proven experience as a Recruiter in a SaaS / Tech / AI environment * Strong expertise in direct sourcing and proactive talent acquisition * Experience recruiting internationally or across multiple regions * Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills * Ability to operate in a fast\-paced, scaling organisation * Fluent in English and French * Experience supporting hypergrowth SaaS companies * Familiarity with roles in AI, data, cloud platforms, or B2B enterprise software * Exposure to hiring Sales and Customer Success roles in EMEA or global markets **Benefits** * Hybrid work model \- a flexible mix of in\-office and remote days * Great culture \- active Social Club organizing regular team events and activities * Health \& wellness \- medical coverage, life insurance, and other wellness programs * Time off \- competitive paid holidays plus public holidays * Career growth \& compensation \- competitive salary, equal opportunities, learning \& mentorship programs, and advancement support Because when you thrive, we all succeed! We're committed to providing a welcoming and inclusive experience for every candidate If you need any accommodation during the hiring process, just let us know. **Agencies** We only accept applications from invited agencies via our Workable portal. Unsolicited CVs sent to managers or HR won't be subject to fees.

title: Recruitment Consultant
description: **Stelfox Tech Recruitment** **Location** \- Dublin 1 (Hybrid / On\-site) **About Us:** This year marks our 25th anniversary\-an exciting milestone as we continue to grow strategically. Stelfox is a leading Technology Talent Company with offices in Galway, Cork and Dublin, and we're looking for an experienced Recruitment Consultant to join our Dublin team. Our goal is to help you realise your full potential. Our culture is people\-first, grounded in mutual respect brought to life through events and social gatherings that connect us. As a Recruitment Consultant at Stelfox, you will be responsible for: * Building and growing a profitable permanent recruitment desk. * Partnering with clients to define hiring requirements, map organisational structures and culture, and align on annual growth plans. * Running proactive candidate sourcing initiatives while developing and converting new business opportunities. * Maintaining regular candidate and client engagement, including organising networking events and delivering client presentations. * Negotiating commercial terms and agreements with clients. * Cross\-selling additional disciplines and introduce wider group services to deepen and expand client relationships. **What We're Looking For** * 2\-5 years' experience technology recruitment in an agency recruitment environment. * Full recruitment lifecycle experience, from sourcing through to placement. * Proven ability to manage client relationships, win new business, and maximise existing accounts. * Target\-driven, goal\-oriented mindset. * Collaborative team player who can also work independently and take ownership. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * Confident, professional approach with strong stakeholder presence. * Strong drive to succeed, with the commitment and resilience required to thrive in a sales\-led role. * Demonstrated history of high performance and achievement in previous roles. **How to Apply:** If you're an experienced Recruitment Consultant and ready to help drive the next phase of Stelfox's growth, please submit your CV and a cover letter outlining your relevant experience and why you're a strong fit for the role to Managing Director Jennifer Dillon at jennifer@stelfox.com.

title: Recruitment Specialist (Accepting All Backgrounds)
description: * Join a booming industry in Dublin and **become a Recruitment Specialist in 2 years.** * **No recruitment experience needed** – you will learn everything from our top performers and directors. * **Join an ambitious, international** team where every day is about growth, impact, and celebrating success. **Who** Are We? Access Healthcare is one of Ireland’s fastest\-growing recruitment consultancies, delivering top\-tier talent across the healthcare industry. But growth alone isn’t what defines us. We’re building something different. A business where performance drives progression. Where ambition is matched with real opportunity. People are at the core of everything we do. We invest heavily in training, mentorship, and internal progression \- giving you the tools and support to grow fast and succeed. **What makes Access Healthcare different?** From day one, you’ll be trusted with real responsibility, supported by experienced leaders, and encouraged to take ownership of your growth. This is a place for ambitious individuals who want to make an impact and build a long\-term career. **🌟 A Day in the Life – 6 Months In 🌟** No two days are the same. Mornings start with coffee and a quick team huddle to set goals, then it’s straight into **live search assignments, writing standout job ads, and keeping multiple processes moving at pace** . The rest of the day is about **people, momentum, and results** \- meeting ambitious candidates, partnering with clients, prepping talent for interviews, and celebrating wins as deals come together. Along the way, you’ll level up your skills through **Certified Recruitment Consultant (CRC) Training** , before wrapping up with a high\-energy team round\-up full of laughs, support, and shared wins. **What’s in it for You?** * **Expert mentorship \& training** – Learn from the best in the market and skyrocket your career. * **Long\-term growth** – We invest in you from day one, aiming to help you reach senior and leadership roles. * **Merit\-based success** – Your effort drives your progress. The harder you work, the faster you climb. * **Fast promotions** – Most consultants get promoted in **6 months** , top performers in just 3\. How fast do you want to rise? * **Friday fun** – Kick back with a fully stocked beer fridge and after\-work bonding with your team. **Our Social Life** It’s not all work. Our dedicated social committee organises events all year: outdoor adventures, quizzes, nights out, welness activities and more! **Salary \& Earning Potential** We offer a base salary plus uncapped commissions \- your earning potential is unlimited: * Average performers earn around €45k in year one (base \+ commission) * Year two typically rises to €75k For more information, email your CV at francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie No prior recruitment experience is required \- just the motivation to learn and succeed.

title: Registrar in Haematology (Beaumont/Connolly) - July 2026
description: Overview: **Overview** ------------ **BEAUMONT HOSPITAL****(Incorporating St Joseph’s Hospital, Raheny)** Beaumont Hospital, incorporating St. Joseph’s Hospital Raheny, is a large academic teaching hospital 5km north of Dublin City centre. Beaumont Hospital provides emergency and acute care services across 54 medical specialties to a local community of some 290,000 people. In addition, Beaumont Hospital is a designated cancer centre and the regional treatment centre for ear, nose and throat, and gastroenterology, and the national referral centre for neurosurgery and neurology, renal transplantation, cochlear implantation and mechanical thrombectomy. Beaumont Hospital is the lead Level 4 hospital within the Royal College of Surgeons Hospital Group, which includes Connolly Hospital, Rotunda Hospital, Cavan/Monaghan Hospitals, Louth/Meath Hospitals, and the Royal College of Surgeons. Beaumont Hospital employs approximately 4,000 staff and has a bed complement of 820 beds. Beaumont Hospital is the principal teaching hospital for the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland. Beaumont Hospital also enjoys close links with Dublin City University, especially in the area of nurse training, and with other academic institutions in respect of training and research. St. Joseph's Hospital Raheny, is an acute hospital that provides both medical and surgical in\-patient care, day care, outpatient, outpatient physiotherapy and radiology services. A dedicated Care of the Elderly Rehabilitation Unit and a 100 bedded Raheny Community Nursing Unit have also been developed on the St. Joseph’s campus. **Applications are invited from suitably qualified doctors for the following post which is available from 13th July 2026 for 6 months** **Registrar in Haematology (Beaumont/Connolly)** Post Specifications: * Each candidate **must** hold a valid **Irish Medical Council Registration** * English Language Competency: All doctors taking up employment who were not registered with the Irish Medical Council in any of the divisions of the Register of Medical Practitioners prior to **1st January 2015**, or who did not complete the entirety of their under\-graduate medical training in the Republic of Ireland, are required to provide evidence of one of the following: * Completion of your Medical degree in English from any of the following countries: Australia, Canada, New Zealand, The United States of America or The United Kingdom. A copy of your Degree Certificate is the required evidence **OR*** English Language Tests Certificate: International English Language Test System Certificate (IELTS – minimum overall band score of 7\.0\) **or** Occupational English Test Certificate (OET – minimum overall grade score of B). IELTS/OET test results must be current and dated within two years of the date submitted to the employer. **Closing date for receipt of applications is 9am on Monday 18th May 2026\.** Applicants should note that it will not be possible to inform all applicants individually of the outcome of their applications. **Please note that some of the above posts may be filled by recognised training schemes prior to any hospital interviews.****Beaumont Hospital is an equal opportunities employer and supports a no smoking policy.**

title: Renewable Energy Specialist
description: **Project Manager (Lead Large\-Scale Renewable Energy Infrastructure Projects) – PMO** \- through all phases of development including **Planning, Commercialisation, Construction, Commissioning** and **Operation** Location: **Newbridge, Co. Kildare, Ireland** (Hybrid working model with blended **working from home for three days** and **two days in the office** ) Duration: **Permanent** In partnership with our client is a large **Irish Semi‑State Organisation** , headquartered in Newbridge, Co. Kildare, has evolved into a modern **Climate‑Action Enterprise** . It now focuses on **Renewable Energy Development** , rehabilitation, waste and resource recovery, and **Large‑Scale Environmental Restoration projects.** Its mission centres on supporting Ireland’s transition to a **Climate‑Neutral Economy** by **2050** through **Clean‑Energy Solutions** , Biodiversity Regeneration, and Sustainable Land Management. They are hiring a **Project Manager** . The **Project Manager** is responsible for delivering their **Large\-Scale Renewable Energy Projects** , managing all aspects of **Project Development** through to operation. Working within the **Renewable Energy program structure** , the role leads individual project delivery, ensuring **Commercial, Technical** , and **Financial Success** in line with their governance and quality standards. The **Project Manager** plays a key part in advancing **Wind, Solar, Battery Storage** , and **Grid Infrastructure Projects** that support their strategic objectives. **The main duties and responsibilities of the role will include the following:** **Lead Large\-Scale Renewable Energy Infrastructure Projects** through all phases of development including **Planning, Commercialisation, Construction, Commissioning** and **Operation.** Coordinate across functional teams in a matrix structure, **leveraging PMO** guidance, technical experts, and operational stakeholders to achieve project objectives. Ensure alignment with their **Strategic Goals, Governance Frameworks** , and **Portfolio Priorities.** Work with their **Functional Leads** and **PMO operations** to **Procure Construction Contracts, Negotiate Long\-Term Offtake Agreements** , and secure **Project Financing.** **Monitor Budgets, Forecasts,** and **Cost Controls** across the **Project Lifecycle** , ensuring financial viability and value for money. Support the **PMO** in delivering accurate portfolio\-level financial reporting and compliance. **Finalise Planning Consents, Permits** , and **Statutory Approvals** required for construction and operational readiness. Ensure adherence to **Regulatory, Environmental** , and **Grid Compliance Requirements** , working collaboratively with functional leads. Identify and mitigate compliance risks proactively, escalating through the **PMO** as needed. Serve as the primary **Project Lead** for internal and external stakeholders, including contractors, regulators, technology suppliers, and community partners. Work within the matrix structure to align cross\-functional teams, ensuring clear roles, responsibilities, and communication channels. Maintain strong collaborative relationships with the **PMO** to facilitate portfolio\-level consistency and delivery standards. Analyse and appraise overall **Project Risk** profiles and provide regular updates so as shareholders are provided with accurate information in which to make their decisions. Prepare all documentation required to obtain investment approvals. Plan and oversee operational handover, ensuring readiness for full\-scale operation and integration with ongoing portfolio management. Provide timely, accurate, and transparent project reporting to the **PMO** and **Program Managers,** supporting portfolio\-level oversight. Maintain project governance in line with **PMO methodologies** , ensuring alignment with company\-wide standards and processes. Capture lessons learned and best practices, feeding back into **PMO** processes to improve overall project delivery capability. All employees are expected to co\-operate fully with all provisions taken by the company for ensuring Health and Safety, and Welfare of co\-employees and members of the public using the premises. Proactively participate in the **Performance Management** process to ensure delivery of own, and the wider team's objectives. What they're looking for: The ideal candidate will possess the following qualification, skills, knowledge, and attributes: A third\-level degree in Engineering, Business, Project Management or a related discipline (Master’s degree advantageous). **Minimum 8 years’ experience** in **Large Scale Energy** or **Infrastructure Project Delivery** , with at **least 4** in a **Junior Project Manager** or **Project Engineer** role within the **Energy Sector.** **Professional Project Management Certification** (e.g. **PMP** or equivalent) is desirable. Strong commercial acumen and experience in budget control, procurement, contract management, and resource optimisation. Familiarity with regulatory, grid, and planning frameworks relevant to **Energy Infrastructure** in Ireland is essential. Demonstrated ability to **Lead Cross Functional Teams** , manage stakeholder relationships, and **Deliver Projects** in complex, multi\-stakeholder settings. In depth knowledge of **Project Management Principles** , with the ability to manage a **Large\-Scale Energy Project** through **Development** and **Construction** using a **Lean** outsourced **Project Management** structure. **Other Benefits Include:** Employer matched pension contributions They invest in their people and have a strong commitment to your development. They provide free, confidential counselling \& wellbeing support service that provides in\-the\-moment support to you. It also provides access to self\-guided meditation, Fitness \& Nutrition Pathways, Digital Gym \& on demand webinars. They fund subscriptions for work\-related professional bodies. Interested? Please send a copy of your CV to **hello@enexa\-partners.com**

title: Resident Engineer - MEICA
description: **The Vacancy** **About The Company** At Nicholas O'Dwyer, sustainability is at the core of our work. If you are looking to advance your career in a socially responsible company, which has a proven track record over 90 years, and is committed to creating a sustainable planet, we invite you to be part of our team. Our mission is to provide innovative and reliable solutions using cutting edge technology and expertise, to ensure project success and employee satisfaction. We deliver infrastructure projects in water, wastewater, transportation, civil and structural engineering, flood protection, and energy including renewables. With headquarters in Ireland, we also operate in the UK, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. As part of the RSK Group, an amalgamation of over 200 companies with more than 17,000 employees, we benefit from a global network of opportunities while retaining our brand and autonomy. **About the Role:** We currently are seeking Resident Engineers \- MEICA with the preferred candidates having at least 5 years of experience in a similar role. This role provides an exciting opportunity to play a vital role within the site supervision team on Water and Wastewater infrastructure projects in a rapidly growing established Irish\-owned company where efficient procedures and processes, in an ever\-changing business environment, are a must. The successful candidate will be an integral member of our Team and will work with our Staff to ensure the highest quality and standards are achieved in all our activities and services that we provide to our Clients. **Key Duties and Responsibilities:** The successful candidate will, amongst other things, be responsible for: * Reporting to the Senior Resident Engineer and Employer’s Representative. * Liaison with the Site Supervision Teams on each Contract. * Dealing with contractual matters on\-site related to MEICA Works. * Assessment of Contractor claims and writing reports on same. * Assessment of Contractor payment applications against work completed. * Assessment of Contractor performance against Contractual KPIs. * Assessment of Contractor submissions including method statements and preparation of clear comments. * Review Contractor MEICA design submissions including equipment details against client requirements and preparation of clear comments. * Supervise works, keeping records, measuring, managing correspondence, and contractual documentation. * Monitoring the commissioning of the works and process proving. * Other duties as may be assigned. **Key Requirements:** * Primary degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or other relevant disciplines. * 5 to 7 years of relevant experience in major engineering projects. * Strong knowledge of the water industry is preferred. * Familiarity with industry\-standard forms of contract. * Experience in the design, installation, and commissioning of MEICA infrastructure. * Expertise with P\&IDs, Electrical Wiring Diagrams, Control Philosophy, and Factory/Site Acceptance Testing. * Awareness of Health \& Safety and welfare at work requirements during construction and operation. **About Us** Our mission is to deliver sustainable solutions that transform communities and bring about lasting positive change. Through a blend of expertise, innovation and global dedication, we envision a world where sustainable engineering protects growing communities. Nicholas O’Dwyer provides professional services in engineering design, project management, environment and planning, construction supervision and technical and contract advisory. We are one of the most respected Irish consultancies operating in local and international markets, providing professional services to public\- and private\-sector clients. Whether in Ireland, the UK or internationally, we understand and solve major challenges associated with delivering and adapting infrastructure for an increasing global population while maintaining a sustainable environment for future generations. Nicholas O’Dwyer is part of the RSK Group, a leading integrated environmental, engineering, and technical services business providing dynamic, multidisciplinary services to a wide range of sectors. RSK was founded in 1989 and has grown considerably since then. At RSK, we operate as a group of businesses that work together seamlessly, aiming for growth through diversification. Our strategy helps us build a strong and resilient business, capable of weathering external challenges, while also providing greater value to our clients and more opportunities for our employees. Since the beginning, our philosophy has remained the same: employ, develop and reward talented individuals. People are crucial to our company, and we recognise that our success hinges on our colleagues' growth. We're committed to preserving RSK's people\-first culture, promoting innovation, and fostering a supportive and caring environment for collaboration. At RSK, we want you to be part of our successes and help you reach your career aspirations. We take pride in offering employees fulfilling and challenging careers by promoting ongoing growth and empowering individuals to excel. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that welcomes individuals from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We firmly believe that embracing and celebrating the diversity in our workforce makes us a stronger and more innovative company. If you're passionate about making a difference, join us as we work together to build a brighter, more equitable, and sustainable future for all. Do you have what it takes?

title: SMB Account Executive - Africa - English or French Speaker
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Sales Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Note:** By applying to the Small Medium / Mid\-market Business Account Executive posting, recruiters and hiring managers who support multiple cloud offerings and verticals across the organization hiring Small Medium Business Account Executives will review your resume. Our goal is for you to apply once and have your resume reviewed by multiple hiring teams. Our SMB Account Executives engage with existing customers and new leads to sell the entire Salesforce Customer 360 platform. They build positive, trusted relationships with both key team members and decision\-makers within their patch, and become natural at helping customers realize value from their Salesforce investments. You will use your new business development skills to develop opportunities, through both warm leads and whitespace prospecting. **Responsibilities** * Lead a dedicated customer set in the ESMB space being responsible for the full sales cycle. emphasis on new business, while growing existing accounts. * Use your solution selling expertise to respond efficiently to customer needs and identify business potential to create a strategic, long\-term partnership with your customers. * Strengthen client relationships through regular engagement and face\-to\-face meetings * Prioritise and lead industry events and user groups to generate market interest. * Work in partnership with our team of Business Developers, Pre\-sales, and Marketers for all sales leads and sales opportunities. **Required Skills And Qualifications** * Demonstrated success of quota carrying, technology solution\-based direct sales experience * Territory \& Account Planning Strategies: Create territory and account plans to achieve sales objectives by effectively identifying and qualifying prospects and opportunities. * Research and Discovery: Uncovers a prospect’s current processes, business challenges, and strategic goals based on customer use cases and value hypotheses. * Solutioning: Identifies compelling value propositions that address customer needs by demonstrating an understanding of technology solutions. * Customer Communication: Interacting with customers in a clear, concise, and timely manner using a variety of communication methods (writing, speech, presentation) and tools (Whiteboarding, Slack, Google Slides, Zoom). * Resource Application: Continuously runs toward results using the full capabilities of available resources and tools. * Team Selling: Aligns with the full capacities of the account team and partners to support the deal and customer success. **Preferred Skills And Qualifications** * Excellent interpersonal and communications skills. * Sales Methodology education. * Ability to develop cases and service requirements, while crafting and leading strategic alliances. * Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Track record of consistently achieving or surpassing quota. * Experience will be evaluated based on alignment to the core competencies for the role (e.g. extracurricular leadership roles, military experience, volunteer work, etc.). * Fluent in English or French **Benefits \& Perks** Check out our benefits site which explains our various benefits, including well\-being reimbursement, generous parental leave, adoption assistance, fertility benefits, and more **Accommodations** If you require assistance due to a disability applying for open positions please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. **Posting Statement** At Salesforce we believe that the business of business is to improve the state of our world. Each of us has a responsibility to drive Equality in our communities and workplaces. We are committed to creating a workforce that reflects society through inclusive programs and initiatives such as equal pay, employee resource groups, inclusive benefits, and more. Learn more about Equality at Salesforce and explore our benefits. Salesforce is an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Salesforce does not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Salesforce will not pay any third\-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Salesforce. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination.
